1000677522.english: - I began explaining last night's events to Konstantscha.
1000677522.korean: - 나는 콘스탄챠에게 어젯밤에 있던 일을 설명하기 시작했다.

1000677523.english: - It was a late night. Clouds may have covered the moon, or the moon itself may have hidden in fear of the darkness; not even moonlight could be seen.
1000677523.korean: - 야심한 밤이다. 구름이 달을 가렸는지, 혹은 달마저 어둠이 무서워 숨었는지, 달빛조차 보이지 않을 정도로 캄캄한 밤.

1000677524.english: - In such a midnight, someone was walking through the corridors of Orca...
1000677524.korean: - 그런 심야에, 오르카 호의 복도를 돌아다니는 이가 있었으니...

1000677525.english: - What am I hiding? It's me.

1000677526.english: - In my home-like Orca, I was tiptoeing like a thief because...
1000677526.korean: - 내 집이나 다름없는 오르카 호에서, 내가 좀도둑처럼 살금살금 발끝으로 걸어다니고 있는 이유는 바로...

1000677527.english: - I was looking for my precious treasure.

1000677529.english: - If caught working all night again, I might have to say goodbye to night shifts forever...
1000677529.korean: - 또 밤새서 일하다가 걸리면 철야업무와는 영영 작별해야 할 수도 있겠지만...

1000677530.english: - But this time I prepared the perfect excuse.

1000677531.english: Hmm, if staying up all night working displeases you...
1000677531.korean: 음, 밤을 새서 일하는 걸 못마땅해 하신다면...

1000677532.english: Don't make an excuse about another reason for staying up.
1000677532.korean: 다른 이유로 밤을 샜다고 핑계를 대시지 말임다.

1000677533.english: Just say you stayed up playing Steel Line Online.
1000677533.korean: 밤새서 스틸라인 온라인을 했다고 하면 되는 것 아닙니까?

1000677534.english: Hah, my nickname is actually "the Cunning of Steel Line."
1000677534.korean: 훗, 사실 제 별명이 스틸라인의 꾀주머니임다.

1000677535.english: Even Draco acknowledged it and gave me that nickname.
1000677535.korean: 드라코도 절 인정해서 이런 별명을 붙여줬을 정도임다.

1000677536.english: - So, next time you need my help, don't hesitate to ask.
1000677536.korean: 그러니, 다음에도 제 도움이 필요하시다면 언제든지 말씀해주시지 말임다.

1000677537.english: - Thanks to Brownie's brilliant scheme, even safety measures were prepared...
1000677537.korean: - 브라우니의 천재적인 계략으로 안전장치를 마련하기까지 했으니...

1000677538.english: - Now I can stay up all night doing paperwork as much as I want!
1000677538.korean: - 이제 얼마든지 밤을 새서 서류작업을 할 수 있다!

1000677539.english: - I chuckled and returned to my room.
1000677539.korean: - 나는 흐흐 웃음을 흘리며 방으로 돌아왔다.

1000677540.english: - Sekhmet and Armand's worried faces flickered before my eyes, but I steeled my mind.
1000677540.korean: - 걱정스러운 표정을 짓는 세크메트와 아르망의 얼굴이 눈 앞에 아른거렸지만, 나는 마음을 굳게 먹기로 했다.

1000677541.english: - This is something that can't be helped.

1000677543.english: - Wait, who was I just talking to...?
1000677543.korean: - 잠깐, 방금 내가 대화하고 있던 게... 누구지?

1000677544.english: - There was someone else in my room besides me...!
1000677544.korean: - 내 방에 나 말고 다른 사람이 있었다...!

1000677545.english: - Today's guard for me is Fero. So, no dangerous person would be let in. But...
1000677545.korean: - 오늘 내 경호를 맡은 건 페로. 그러니, 위험한 사람을 들여보낼 리는 없다. 하지만...

1000677546.english: - No one would stop someone from confiscating my pad like Cerestia and Alpha would!
1000677546.korean: - 세레스티아나 알파처럼 내 패드를 압수할 사람을 막지는 않을 터!

1000677547.english: - I quickly launched Steel Line Online and said.
1000677547.korean: - 나는 잽싸게 스틸라인 온라인을 켜며 말했다.

1000677549.english: ...Ha, how blatantly obvious an excuse to give.
1000677549.korean: ...하아, 어쩜 그리 뻔히 보이는 핑계를 대는 건지.

1000677550.english: I knew you were a workaholic, but this is truly impressive...
1000677550.korean: 워커홀릭인건 알고 있었지만, 진짜 감탄이 나올 정도네...

1000677552.english: - I couldn't help but be surprised to see a face I never expected in my room.
1000677552.korean: - 내 방에서 볼 거라고는 생각조차 하지 못한 얼굴을 보게 되자 놀랄 수밖에 없었다.

1000677553.english: I'm not taking it, so put that aside.
1000677553.korean: 안 뺏어가니까, 그거 저리 치워 봐.

1000677556.english: - You're not talking about a Steel Line Online party, right?
1000677556.korean: - 스틸라인 온라인 파티를 말하는 건 아닐테고.

1000677557.english: I want to join the Blind Princess rescue team too.
1000677557.korean: 블라인드 프린세스 구출팀, 나도 같이 가고 싶다고.

1000677558.english: - That was truly unexpected, so I had no choice but to answer this way.
1000677558.korean: - 그건 정말 예상하지 못한 용건이었기에, 나는 이렇게 대답할 수밖에 없었다.

1000677559.english: I have something to ask the Blind Princess.
1000677559.korean: 블라인드 프린세스에게 묻고 싶은 게 있어.

1000677560.english: It's... a question about the past.

1000677561.english: - Since it seemed like it would be a long story, I stood up and opened the door.
1000677561.korean: - 꽤나 긴 이야기가 될 것 같았기에, 나는 자리에서 일어나 방 문을 열었다.

1000677562.english: Good. That will help organize my thoughts.
1000677562.korean: 좋아. 그 편이 머리 속을 정리하기도 더 좋겠지.

1000677567.english: - Surtr… that's the name of the fire giant from Norse mythology.
1000677567.korean: - 수르트라면... 북유럽 신화에 나오는 불의 거인의 이름이구나.

1000677568.english: In the movie created by Densez and Bismark, "Dragon Slayer 2," I was supposed to appear as the villain.
1000677568.korean: 덴세츠와 비스마르크가 힘을 합쳐 만든 영화, ‘드래곤 슬레이어’ 2편의 빌런으로 등장하게 될 예정이었지.

1000677569.english: The reason my staff is called Laevatein, known in popular media as Surtr's sword, or the wolf that accompanies me is named Garm, the gatekeeper of hell, is all because of that.
1000677569.korean: 내 지팡이 이름이 흔히 대중매체에서 수르트의 검이라고 알려진 레바테인이라거나, 나와 같이 다니는 늑대의 이름이 지옥의 문지기인 가름이라거나 하는 것도 다 그 때문이야.

1000677570.english: After the huge success of Dragon Slayer, Bismark wanted to produce a sequel.
1000677570.korean: 드래곤 슬레이어의 대흥행 이후, 비스마르크에서는 드래곤 슬레이어의 속편을 제작하려고 했어.

1000677571.english: But the problem was that there was no villain to appear in the sequel.
1000677571.korean: 하지만, 속편에서 등장할 악당이 없는 게 문제였지.

1000677572.english: Though there was still the battle with Nidhogg left, Nidhogg felt like the final boss that ends the series the moment it falls.
1000677572.korean: 니드호그와의 싸움이 아직 남아있긴 했어도, 니드호그는 쓰러지는 순간 시리즈가 마무리되는 최종 보스라는 느낌이었으니까.

1000677573.english: Without a proper idea, Bismark opened a "Villain Contest" under the pretext of celebrating the success of the first movie… and thanking the fans.
1000677573.korean: 마땅한 아이디어가 없던 비스마르크는 1편의 성공을 기념한다는 명목으로... 또 팬들에게 감사한다는 핑계로, ‘빌런 공모전’을 열었어.

1000677574.english: That's when the lab director who created me appeared.
1000677574.korean: 거기서 날 만든 연구소장이 등장하는 거야.

1000677575.english: The lab director was eccentric. And a huge fan of Dragon Slayer.
1000677575.korean: 연구소장은 괴짜였어. 그리고 드래곤 슬레이어의 광팬이었지.

1000677576.english: So when the lab director heard about the contest, he must have thought,
1000677576.korean: 그래서, 연구소장은 공모전 소식을 들었을 때, 이런 생각을 했을거야.

1000677577.english: What? A contest? I can't miss this!
1000677577.korean: 뭐? 공모전? 내가 여기에 빠질 수야 없지!

1000677578.english: But that's a problem… I'm not good at drawing…
1000677578.korean: 그런데 큰일이네… 그림은 잘 못 그리는데...

1000677579.english: ...Wait, is there even a need to draw?

1000677580.english: Why not just create a bio-roid from the start and send it?
1000677580.korean: 처음부터 바이오로이드를 만들어서 보내면 되는 거 아니야?

1000677581.english: Yes! This is it! Create a bio-roid and send it!
1000677581.korean: 그래! 바로 이거다! 바이오로이드를 만들어서 보내는 거야!

1000677582.english: The grand prize of the contest is mine!

1000677584.english: But the person was definitely capable. They created me alone.
1000677584.korean: 하지만, 능력은 확실한 사람이었어. 혼자서 날 만들어냈거든.

1000677585.english: Taking inspiration from the withered Muromachi flower… and fire from bio-electricity…
1000677585.korean: 시들어 버린 무로마치의 꽃에서 모티브를 따와서… 생체전기로 불을…

1000677587.english: Every compelling story needs a compelling villain...
1000677587.korean: 매력적인 이야기에는 매력적인 악역이 필요한 법...

1000677588.english: Surtr, you're going to be the most charming villain ever!
1000677588.korean: 수르트, 너는 정말 최고로 매력적인 악역이 될 거야!

1000677589.english: I was supposed to meet my end as the villain of the Dragon Slayer series.
1000677589.korean: 나는 그렇게 드래곤 슬레이어 시리즈의 빌런으로 죽음을 맞이할 예정이었어.

1000677590.english: To prevent the technology used to create me from being leaked, I was supposed to spectacularly explode… all contracts with Bismark were already finalized.
1000677590.korean: 나를 만드는 데에 쓰인 기술이 유출되는 것을 방지하기 위해, 나는 화려하게 폭사하는 것으로... 비스마르크와 계약까지 전부 끝난 상태였지.

1000677591.english: Yes. One day, the lab director stomped into the room.
1000677591.korean: 그래. 어느 날, 연구소장이 씩씩대면서 방 안으로 들어오더라고.

1000677593.english: You're no longer Surtr. You're Brünhild, a bio-roid I created on my own whim because I thought it'd be fun. Got it?
1000677593.korean: 넌 이제부터 수르트가 아니야. 넌 브륀힐드고, 내가 재미있을 것 같아서 독단으로 만든 바이오로이드야. 알았어?

1000677594.english: I said I got it, but one question inevitably arose.
1000677594.korean: 알았다고는 대답했지만, 한 가지 의문이 생기는 건 어쩔 수 없었지.

1000677595.english: I asked, "Why?" The lab director said:
1000677595.korean: 나는 물었어. ‘왜 그러는데?’ 하고 말이야. 연구소장은 이렇게 말하더군.

1000677596.english: The Blind Princess refused to film a movie with you.
1000677596.korean: 블라인드 프린세스가 너와 함께 영화를 찍는 걸 거절했어.

1000677597.english: Originally, you should have been disposed of on the spot to prevent technology leakage, but…
1000677597.korean: 원래대로라면 넌 기술유출을 막기 위해 그 자리에서 처분되어야 했지만...

1000677598.english: I couldn't let my creation meet such an ending.
1000677598.korean: 내 작품이 그딴 결말을 맞이하게 둘 수는 없지.

1000677599.english: So you'll live and work with me in the research facility from now on. Understood?
1000677599.korean: 그러니까 넌 앞으로 연구시설에서 나랑 같이 생활해야 해. 알겠어?

1000677600.english: - Thanks to that, I survived, but ever since, I've always wondered. Why did that happen? Why did the Blind Princess refuse to film with me?
1000677600.korean: 덕분에 목숨은 건졌지만, 난... 그 뒤로 항상 의문을 가지고 있었어. 왜 그랬을까? 왜 블라인드 프린세스는 나와 함께 영화를 찍는 걸 거절했을까?

1000677602.english: - When I came to my senses, we were walking along the sandy shore.
1000677602.korean: - 정신을 차려보니, 어느새 우리는 바닷가의 백사장을 걷고 있었다.

1000677603.english: - Brünhild stopped speaking and looked up at the night sky.

1000677604.english: - She stared at the dark sky, devoid of moon or stars, for a long time before turning her gaze to me.
1000677604.korean: - 달도, 별도 없는 어두운 하늘을 한참동안 바라보던 그녀는 내게 시선을 옮겼다.

1000677605.english: Do you think you know the reason?
1000677605.korean: 당신은 그 이유를 알 것 같아?

1000677606.english: - Countless guesses crossed my mind, but I shook my head.
1000677606.korean: - 수많은 추측이 머리 속을 오갔지만, 나는 고개를 저었다.

1000677610.english: Honestly, I didn't think you'd indulge my stubbornness.
1000677610.korean: 솔직히 내 억지를 받아줄 거라고는 생각 못했는데.

1000677611.english: You came unannounced in the middle of the night, causing trouble… I expected you to chase me away.
1000677611.korean: 한밤중에 불쑥 찾아와서 귀찮게 구는 거니까... 방해하지 말라고 내쫓을 줄 알았어.

1000677613.english: By the way, aren't you cold?

1000677614.english: Have you forgotten who's beside you?
1000677614.korean: 당신 옆에 있는 게 누구인지 잊은 거야?

1000677615.english: - Brünhild said that while kindling a small flame in her hand.
1000677615.korean: - 브륀힐드는 그렇게 말하면서 손 위에 불을 피워 올렸다.

1000677619.english: - Though the night was dark without moonlight or starlight…

1000677620.english: - There was a small light to warm ourselves, so I decided to spend a bit more time outside.
1000677620.korean: - 몸을 녹일 자그마한 불빛은 있었으니, 나는 밖에서 조금 더 시간을 보내기로 했다.

1000677622.english: If it's for that reason, it can't be helped.
1000677622.korean: 그런 이유 때문이라면 어쩔 수 없네요.

1000677623.english: But… now I'm worried for another reason.
1000677623.korean: 하지만... 이제 다른 이유에서 걱정이 되네요.

1000677624.english: I'm the one who's worried about you, master.

1000677626.english: After saying you'd manage your condition, and yet attempting another all-nighter!
1000677626.korean: 컨디션 관리에 힘쓰시겠다고 하고, 이번에도 또 철야를 하시려 하다니!

1000677627.english: I'll manage the pad for the time being.

1000677628.english: - Constantia said that as she took my data panel away. Unfortunately, this makes it nearly impossible to get the panel back for now…
1000677628.korean: - 콘스탄챠는 그렇게 말하며 내 데이터 패널을 가져가 버렸다. 안타깝게도, 이러면 당분간 패널을 되찾는 건 불가능에 가깝다...

1000677629.english: - …I had no choice but to tearfully see off the rescue team.

1000677631.english: How long was the undersea tunnel again? 50 km?

1000677632.english: Considering it's 50km long, I can't believe the ceiling is so low...
1000677632.korean: 길이는 50km나 되는 주제에, 그렇게 천장이 낮다니...

1000677633.english: ...It was a journey that would likely exhaust even Jim, who was accustomed to darkness and shadows.
1000677633.korean: ...어둠과 그림자에 익숙한 짐조차 지칠 법한 여정이었다.

1000677634.english: Especially when my sword got caught while turning a corner, I struggled so much... Ugh.
1000677634.korean: 특히 코너를 돌다가 검이 걸렸을 때는 어찌나 고생했는지... 으으.

1000677635.english: ...Everyone looks really tired. Are you okay?

1000677636.english: How is Mr. Phantom so fine?

1000677637.english: Isn't it only natural? Moving freely in and out of cramped spaces is practically a virtue of an assassin.
1000677637.korean: 당연한 것 아니겠느냐, 비좁은 공간을 자유자재로 드나드는 것은 암살자의 미덕이나 다름없으니.

1000677638.english: ...As you said, I am used to things like this.

1000677639.english: (It was more pleasant than the archives because there was less dust and the space was larger...)
1000677639.korean: (기록물 보관소보다 먼지도 적고 공간도 넓어서 쾌적했는데...)

1000677640.english: Indeed, a shadow minion worthy of the Dragon Slayer's trust.
1000677640.korean: 과연, 용살자의 신뢰를 받을 정도로 훌륭한 그림자의 권속이로다.

1000677641.english: That's amazing. What about Brynhildr and Aias? Are they okay?

1000677644.english: Hmm, anyway, this place... it's a pretty decent-built outpost. Who built this?
1000677644.korean: 흠, 그나저나 여긴... 꽤 그럴싸하게 지어진 전초기지네. 누가 지은 거지?

1000677645.english: Judging by the faint Black River mark remaining, it looks like a Black River military facility.
1000677645.korean: 희미하게 블랙리버의 마크가 남아있는 걸 보면, 블랙리버의 군사시설처럼 보입니다.

1000677646.english: Your observation skills are excellent. You could join City Guard.
1000677646.korean: 관찰력이 아주 훌륭한데? 시티가드에 합류해도 되겠어.

1000677647.english: You are going too far with your jokes.

1000677648.english: Ahaha, I was half-serious, you know? City Guard is always short-staffed.
1000677648.korean: 아하핫, 반쯤은 진담이었는데? 시티가드는 항상 인력부족이라구.

1000677649.english: Then, let's take a short break at the building over there and decide on our next destination.
1000677649.korean: 그럼 일단은 저쪽에 있는 건물에서 잠깐 쉬면서, 다음 목적지를 결정하자.

1000677650.english: Even if we search for missing persons, we have to determine the search area.
1000677650.korean: 실종자 수색을 한다고 해도, 수색 범위를 정해야 하니까.

1000677651.english: We can't search the entire UK to find the missing person, can we?
1000677651.korean: 실종자를 찾기 위해 영국 전체를 다 뒤져볼 수는 없잖아?

1000677652.english: First, let's display a map on the screen... and find a place that meets the criteria.
1000677652.korean: 일단, 스크린에 지도를 띄워 놓고... 조건에 맞는 곳을 찾아 보자.

1000677653.english: So this is a map of the UK.

1000677654.english: Yeah. And this is the predicted deployment map of the Iron Bug troops that Mr. Armand made for me.
1000677654.korean: 응. 그리고 이게 아르망 씨가 만들어주신 철충 병력 배치 예상도야.

1000677655.english: It was created based on the distribution of munitions factories in Black River, so unless there are unusual circumstances, you can assume they are deployed exactly as they are.
1000677655.korean: 블랙리버의 군수공장분포를 토대로 만든 거라서, 특이한 상황이 있지 않다면 이대로 배치되어 있다고 생각해도 될 거야.

1000677656.english: ...There are far too many. Enough to make it difficult even if the entire force of the Orca swarmed in.
1000677656.korean: ...많아도 너무 많군요. 오르카 호의 전 병력이 몰려와도 힘들 정도로.

1000677657.english: Yeah, that's why we ended up coming alone.
1000677657.korean: 응, 그래서 우리만 오게 된 거지.

1000677658.english: Anyway, so... I think we need to have a discussion about 'where the Blind Princess might be.'
1000677658.korean: 아무튼, 그래서... ‘블라인드 프린세스가 어디 있을까’ 를 놓고 논의를 좀 해야 할 것 같아.

1000677659.english: First, I’d like to hear Cyclops Princess’s opinion. Is that okay?
1000677659.korean: 우선 사이클롭스 프린세스 씨의 의견을 듣고 싶은데, 괜찮을까?

1000677660.korean: 흠... 어디 한 번 생각해 보자꾸나.

1000677661.english: Assuming there are no Iron Bug (Iron Bugs), the place most likely to be where the Saint Jim is is London.
1000677661.korean: 철충이 없다고 가정했을 경우, 성녀인 짐이 있을 가능성이 가장 높은 곳은 런던이다.

1000677663.english: Because he was holding an autograph session in London at the time the war broke out. He must have been caught up in the war and trapped in London, unable to go anywhere.
1000677663.korean: 전쟁이 일어난 당시, 런던에서 사인회를 열고 있었으니까. 필시 전쟁에 휘말려서 오도가도 못하고 런던에 갇혔을 게다.

1000677664.english: Is there no possibility that the Blind Princess who held the autograph session and the Blind Princess who just sent us a distress signal are different entities?
1000677664.korean: 사인회를 한 블라인드 프린세스와, 지금 저희에게 구조 신호를 보낸 블라인드 프린세스가 다른 개체일 가능성은 없습니까?

1000677667.english: The value of a gemstone naturally comes from its rarity.
1000677667.korean: 본디 보석의 가치는 그 희귀성에서 나오는 법.

1000677668.english: Jim and Jim the Saint were extremely rare beings. The best and the only.
1000677668.korean: 짐과 성녀인 짐은 극히 희귀한 존재였다. 최고이자 유일한.

1000677669.english: Unless someone created it separately, there is no way for another entity to exist.
1000677669.korean: 누가 따로 만들지 않았다면 다른 개체가 존재할 리가 없지.

1000677670.english: Okay. So, you're saying one of the candidate locations is London...
1000677670.korean: 좋아. 그럼, 후보지 중 하나는 런던이란 말이지...

1000677671.english: Wait! Mr. Phantom, I saw you raise your hand and then lower it just now!
1000677671.korean: 잠깐! 팬텀 씨, 방금 손 들다가 내린 거 다 봤어!

1000677673.english: Hehe, you can't escape a great detective's eyes. Is there something you want to say?
1000677673.korean: 후후, 명탐정의 눈을 피할 수는 없는 법이지. 뭐 하고 싶은 말이라도 있어?

1000677674.english: Hmm... that's right. There is one.

1000677675.english: According to Mr. Armand's map, there are too many Iron Bugs around London.
1000677675.korean: 아르망 씨의 지도에 따르면, 런던 주변에는 철충이 너무 많다.

1000677676.english: don't know much about the Blind Princess, but thinking about it from a tactical perspective...
1000677676.korean: 난 블라인드 프린세스에 대해서는 잘 모르지만, 전술적인 면에서 생각했을 때...

1000677677.english: If there are that many enemies, it is not a good place to use as a base.
1000677677.korean: 적이 저렇게 많다면 근거지로 삼기에 좋은 곳은 아니다.

1000677678.english: Right. So, it's highly likely that it's somewhere else.
1000677678.korean: 그래. 그러니 아마 다른 곳에 있을 가능성이 높지.

1000677679.english: Still, we can narrow down the candidates a bit. Since the last location was London, let's start by searching cities near London.
1000677679.korean: 그래도 후보지를 좀 좁힐 수 있겠네. 마지막 소재지가 런던이었으니, 런던 근처의 도시부터 수색하는 걸로 하자.

1000677680.english: Then other candidate locations would be... Bristol or Plymouth, etc.
1000677680.korean: 그럼 다른 후보지로는... 브리스톨이나 플리머스 등이 있겠네.

1000677682.english: The way the radio is sent is similar to distress signals from ships or aircraft.
1000677682.korean: 무전을 보내는 방식이 배나 항공기의 조난 신호와 유사해.

1000677683.english: Are you talking about the method of repeating Mayday three times?
1000677683.korean: 메이데이를 세 번 반복하는 방식을 말하시는 겁니까?

1000677684.english: Right. So, it's highly likely that a bioroid associated with the Navy or Air Force sent the distress signal instead of the Blind Princess, right? Facilities like that are in Bristol or Plymouth.
1000677684.korean: 그래. 그러니, 아마 해군이나 공군과 관련된 바이오로이드가 블라인드 프린세스 대신 구조 신호를 보냈을 가능성이 높겠지? 그런 시설은 브리스톨이나 플리머스에 있고.

1000677686.english: The signal came through the Black River special security channel. So...
1000677686.korean: 신호는 블랙리버 특수 보안 채널로 날아왔습니다. 그러니...

1000677687.english: So, it’s a Black River naval facility, and it requires the highest security classification?
1000677687.korean: 블랙리버 해군 시설인데다, 최고 보안 등급을 가져야 한다는 건가.

1000677689.english: However, as far as I know, there are no highest-security facilities in Bristol or Plymouth.
1000677689.korean: 하지만 내가 아는 바에 의하면, 브리스톨이나 플리머스에 최고 보안 등급 시설은 없다.

1000677690.english: In that case, the next candidate is...

1000677693.english: The nearest Black River military facility is in Portsmouth.
1000677693.korean: 가장 가까운 블랙리버 군사시설이 포츠머스에 있어.

1000677694.english: No. There is a shipyard in Portsmouth, and there is an old naval base, but...
1000677694.korean: 아니요. 포츠머스에 조선소가 있기도 하고, 옛 해군 기지가 있긴 하지만...

1000677695.english: According to the data from the Arsenal Commander, Black River has never built military facilities in Portsmouth.
1000677695.korean: 아스널 소장님의 데이터에 의하면, 블랙리버는 포츠머스에 군사시설을 건설한 적이 없습니다.

1000677696.english: That might seem that way on the surface.

1000677698.english: This is... a map. What does this map show?

1000677699.english: This is a map showing the location of the Black River secret facility.
1000677699.korean: 블랙리버 기밀 시설의 위치가 표시된 지도야.

1000677700.english: ...Since it is data downloaded from the main computer at the research institute where I was, you can trust it.
1000677700.korean: ...내가 있던 연구소의 메인 컴퓨터에서 다운받았던 데이터니까, 믿어도 될 거야.

1000677701.english: How do you have such data?
1000677701.korean: 그런 데이터를 어떻게 가지고 있는 거죠?

1000677702.english: That is true. The data the Bermuda team possessed was a top secret among top secrets.
1000677702.korean: 그 말대로다. 버뮤다 팀이 가지고 있던 데이터는 기밀 중의 기밀.

1000677703.english: Unless you are at the level of a research institute director, you wouldn't even be able to view confidential data...
1000677703.korean: 연구소장 급이 아니면 기밀 데이터는 열람조차 하지 못할 텐데...

1000677704.english: Well, everyone has their reasons. Anyway, the important thing is that this data exists, isn't it?
1000677704.korean: 뭐, 다 사정이 있어. 아무튼 중요한 건 이 데이터가 있다는 거 아니겠어?

1000677706.english: Anyway, look at this. The Black River Building in Portsmouth.
1000677706.korean: 아무튼, 이걸 봐. 포츠머스에 있는 블랙리버 빌딩.

1000677707.english: This is actually Black River's secret fortress.

1000677708.english: "Camelot." I don't know who named it, but it looks like a name given by someone who really loves the King Arthur legend.
1000677708.korean: ‘카멜롯’이라. 누군지는 몰라도, 아서 왕 전설을 참 좋아하는 사람이 지은 이름인 것 같네.

1000677709.english: Alright... Battle Fortress Camelot. Let's see...
1000677709.korean: 좋아... 전투요새 카멜롯. 어디 한 번 볼까...

1000677710.english: It seems to meet all the conditions to serve as a base for the Blind Princess. It isn't too far from London, and it has facilities for sending radio signals. It is somewhat self-sufficient, and its defenses are formidable.
1000677710.korean: 블라인드 프린세스의 근거지가 될 만한 모든 조건을 충족하는 것 같네. 런던과 그리 멀지 않고, 무전을 보낼 만한 시설도 있어. 어느 정도 자급자족도 가능하고, 방어시설도 엄청나.

1000677711.english: If you travel from Dover via the coastal road, you can arrive today.
1000677711.korean: 도버에서 해안 도로를 통해 이동한다면 오늘 내로 도착할 수 있습니다.

1000677712.english: Right. But there is one problem. If you travel via the coastal road, you will run right into the Iron Bug's coastal defense forces.
1000677712.korean: 그래. 하지만 한 가지 문제가 있어. 해안 도로를 통해 이동하면 철충의 해안 방어 병력이랑 그대로 마주치게 되거든.

1000677713.english: So, we took the M20 highway to approach London, where there are relatively few Iron Bug (Iron Bugs)...
1000677713.korean: 그러니까, 우리는 M20 고속도로를 타고 철충이 비교적 적은 런던으로 접근해서...

1000677714.english: I plan to go around the outskirts of London and then head south to Portsmouth from there.
1000677714.korean: 런던 외곽을 빙 돌아 이동하고, 거기서 포츠머스로 남하할 계획이야.

1000677715.english: Hmm, that sounds like a good plan. But I think I need to warm up a bit.
1000677715.korean: 흠, 좋은 계획인 것 같네. 근데 난 좀 몸을 풀어야 할 것 같거든?

1000677716.english: So, while you guys rest for a bit, I'll go scout the highway in advance.
1000677716.korean: 그러니까 잠시 너희가 쉬고 있는 동안, 내가 미리 고속도로를 정찰하고 올 게.

1000677717.english: Wait, don't go alone; at least two people move together.
1000677717.korean: 잠깐, 혼자 가지 말고, 최소한 두 사람이 같이 움직여.

1000677718.english: If by any chance the missing person is found, one person needs to return to us to report the news. We can't communicate via radio, you know?
1000677718.korean: 혹시나 실종자를 찾았을 경우, 한 명은 우리에게 돌아와서 실종자를 찾았다는 소식을 알려야 하니까. 무전이 안 되잖아?

1000677719.english: ...I see. In that case, the person going with me is...

1000677720.english: I will take my luggage with me.

1000677721.english: Why are you acting like this? Are you doubting my abilities?
1000677721.korean: 왜 그러지? 짐의 실력을 의심하는 것이더냐?

1000677722.english: No, no. It was just unexpected.

1000677723.english: Then, shall we take a look at the skills of the Jinjo Princess?
1000677723.korean: 그럼 어디 한 번 진조의 공주님 실력이나 볼까?

1000677725.english: Well, for now, we've burned everything visible… nothing alive seems to be on the road except us.
1000677725.korean: 글쎄, 하지만 일단 보이는 건 다 태워버렸으니... 도로 위에 살아 움직이는 건 우리들 빼고는 없는 것 같네.

1000677727.english: Aren't there… quite a few more of the Metal Parasites than expected?

1000677729.english: It means the actual Metal Parasite deployment differs significantly from expectations…
1000677729.korean: 실제 철충의 병력 배치가, 예상과는 많이 다르다는 얘긴데...

1000677731.english: More likely… an unexpected variable occurred.
1000677731.korean: 그보다는... 예상하지 못한 변수가 일어났을 가능성이 높겠지.

1000677735.english: Is that so. Well, it can't be helped.
1000677735.korean: 그런가. 뭐, 어쩔 수 없는 노릇이구나.

1000677736.english: Then let's take a short break and head back.

1000677737.english: No. I'll look around a bit more.
1000677737.korean: 아니. 난 좀 더 주위를 돌아볼게.

1000677738.english: ...I want to find the Blind Princess quickly.

1000677740.english: Ha, even after seeing this scene, you don't trust me? Don't you see who burned all the enemies to ashes?
1000677740.korean: 하, 이 광경을 보고도 날 못 믿는 거야? 적들을 다 태워 죽인 게 누구인지 안 보여?

1000677741.english: Well, it seems you have your reasons too...
1000677741.korean: 뭐, 네게도 사정이 있는 것 같다만...

1000677742.english: There might be a second wave of the Metal Parasites, and anything could happen during battle, so I can't send you alone.
1000677742.korean: 철충의 후발대가 있을지도 모르고, 전투 중에 어떤 불상사가 일어날지도 모르는 일이니, 혼자 보낼 수는 없노라.

1000677743.english: Don't worry, these guys will just turn to ashes in front of my fire.
1000677743.korean: 걱정 마, 이깟 놈들은 내 불 앞에 다 잿더미가 될 뿐이니까.

1000677744.english: Hmm... I understand your impatience, but reducing the number of Metal Parasites here won't make you meet the Blind Princess any faster.
1000677744.korean: 으음... 조급한 건 알겠지만, 여기서 철충의 수를 좀 줄여둔다고 블라인드 프린세스와 빨리 만날 수 있는 건 아니니라.

1000677745.english: So calm down. I can see the tension in your shoulders even from afar.
1000677745.korean: 그러니 좀 진정하도록 해라. 어깨에 힘이 들어간 게 멀리서도 보일 정도니까.

1000677747.english: And, don't you need time to stop that prickly act and relax too?
1000677747.korean: 그리고, 너도 그 까칠한 연기를 그만두고 편하게 있을 시간이 필요하지 않더냐?

1000677748.english: Huh...? Act? I don't know what you're talking about.
1000677748.korean: 하...? 연기라니... 무슨 말을 하는지 모르겠네?

1000677749.english: Hmph, I can easily see through lies like that.
1000677749.korean: 훗, 짐의 사안은 그런 거짓 정도는 손쉽게 꿰뚫어 볼 수 있노라.

1000677750.english: If we talk about acting experience, I've got more than you anyway.
1000677750.korean: 연기 경력이라면 짐이 더 길기도 하고 말이다.

1000677751.english: Yeah, maybe so. But what can I do? Everything about acting or whatever is just your illusion.
1000677751.korean: 그래, 뭐 그렇겠지. 그런데 어쩌나? 연기니 뭐니 하는 건 전부 네 착각일 뿐이거든.

1000677752.english: If you don't want to spill it now, I won't press further...
1000677752.korean: 굳이 지금 털어놓고 싶지 않다면, 짐도 더 캐묻지 않겠다만...

1000677753.english: But if you ever feel like it later, come to my room.
1000677753.korean: 혹시나 나중에라도 그럴 마음이 들었다면, 짐의 방으로 찾아오거라.

1000677754.english: Even if I don't have a counselor license, I can certainly listen to your worries.
1000677754.korean: 짐이 상담사 자격증은 없어도, 고민 상담 정도야 얼마든지 해 줄 수 있으니.

1000677755.english: Ha, you say something similar to that Dragon Slayer fellow.
1000677755.korean: 하, 그 용살자 씨랑 비슷한 소리를 하네.

1000677756.english: Ahem, isn't that obvious? The Dragon Slayer and I are bound by the thread of red fate.
1000677756.korean: 크흠, 그거야 당연하지 않느냐. 용살자와 짐은 붉은 운명의 실로 엮인 사이니까.

1000677757.english: It's only natural to say similar things!
1000677757.korean: 비슷한 소리를 하는 것도 당연한 일이다!

1000677763.english: Pretending to dodge when you already plan to accept it.

1000677764.english: You were a bit embarrassed to accept my offer outright, huh?
1000677764.korean: 짐의 제안을 덥썩 받아들이기는 좀 부끄러웠던 모양이지?

1000677768.english: Fine, fine. I already know everything.
1000677768.korean: 그럼, 그럼. 짐도 다 알고 있다.

1000677770.english: I'll go ahead first, follow or not, it's up to you!
1000677770.korean: 난 먼저 갈 거니까, 따라오던지 말던지 마음대로 해!

1000677773.english: Ah, you're back. Good work. How was the reconnaissance?
1000677773.korean: 아, 왔구나. 고생했어. 정찰 결과는 어땠어?

1000677776.english: One thing… except that there were too few enemies.
1000677776.korean: 한 가지... 적들이 너무 적었다는 것만 빼면.

1000677779.english: Anyway, thanks for the reconnaissance. I'll be mindful of the low number of Metal Parasites.
1000677779.korean: 일단, 정찰 고마워. 철충의 수가 너무 적다는 거, 주의하고 있을게.

1000677780.english: Then let's head straight to London!

1000677781.english: We barely encountered any Metal Parasites at all.
1000677781.korean: 정말 철충을 거의 못 만나고 왔네.

1000677782.english: We encountered about 80% fewer Metal Parasites than expected.
1000677782.korean: 예상했던 수보다 80% 가량 적은 철충을 만났습니다.

1000677784.english: Well... we'll know once we head to Portsmouth.
1000677784.korean: 글쎄... 일단은 포츠머스로 향하면 알 수 있겠지.

1000677788.english: Are you intimidated by my presence? Don't hide, come out!
1000677788.korean: 짐의 위광에 겁이라도 먹었느냐? 숨지 말고 나오거라!

1000677792.english: As you can see, I'm a body that cannot be seen.

1000677794.english: Hehe, that voice… it's you, Chi? I didn't expect you to be in England.
1000677794.korean: 후후, 이 목소리... 작은 저군요? 영국에 있을 줄은 몰랐는데.

1000677795.english: Chi… hmm, not wrong, but strangely annoying.
1000677795.korean: 작은... 으음, 틀린 말은 아니지만, 묘하게 짜증나는데.

1000677796.english: Anyway, it seems there's no need to go to Portsmouth.
1000677796.korean: 어쨌거나, 아무래도 포츠머스로 갈 필요는 없겠구나.

1000677798.english: Because that woman is another side of me, the True Saint…
1000677798.korean: 왜냐하면, 저 여자가 바로 짐의 또 다른 이면, 진조의 성녀...

1000677800.english: The Blind Princess? But why is she in London, not Portsmouth?
1000677800.korean: 블라인드 프린세스라고? 하지만, 어째서 포츠머스가 아니라 런던에 있지?

1000677801.english: Portsmouth, I came because I saw the Resistance's notice.
1000677801.korean: 포츠머스라, 저항군 공고를 보고 찾아오신 모양이네요.

1000677802.english: As you say, I was in Portsmouth until recently. But…
1000677802.korean: 그 말씀대로, 저는 얼마 전 까지만 해도 포츠머스에 있었습니다. 하지만...

1000677805.english: It's too long a story to tell here… shall we sit down somewhere to talk?
1000677805.korean: 여기서 이야기하기에는 너무 긴 이야기니까... 어디 앉아서 대화하지 않으실래요?

1000677806.english: Alright. You can come to our Resistance's temporary headquarters.
1000677806.korean: 그래. 저희 저항군의 임시 본부로 오시면 되겠군요.

1000677807.english: It's not much, but we have food and medicine.

1000677808.english: Hmm. Let's do that for now.

1000677809.english: Then guide us, into the city.

1000677810.english: Now, this is the quarters currently being used by the resistance.
1000677810.korean: 자, 여기가 바로 저항군이 현재 사용중인 숙소랍니다.

1000677812.english: It used to be a hospital.

1000677813.english: If you happen to have any pain, please let our medication manager know.
1000677813.korean: 혹시 아프신 곳이라도 있다면, 저희 의약품 관리자에게 말씀해 주세요.

1000677815.english: More importantly, what happened with the Portsmouth matter?
1000677815.korean: 그보다, 포츠머스 건은 어떻게 된 일인가요?

1000677817.english: It’s a bit embarrassing to say this myself, but... I was Bismarck’s popular bioroid.
1000677817.korean: 제 입으로 말하기는 조금 민망하지만... 저는 비스마르크의 인기 바이오로이드였습니다.

1000677818.english: So, after the movie became a huge hit, I had to tour around the world and participate in fan signing events.
1000677818.korean: 그래서, 영화의 대 히트 이후 세계 각지를 순회하며 팬 사인회에 참가해야 했답니다.

1000677819.english: It was truly a rewarding time.

1000677820.english: The children chanting Jim's name, and the flashes going off everywhere... they still remain in my memory.
1000677820.korean: 짐의 이름을 연호하는 아이들과, 여기저기서 터지는 플래시... 아직도 기억 속에 남아 있다.

1000677821.english: Haha, yes. It was a good time.

1000677822.english: It would have been really nice if the Second Coalition War hadn't broken out in the meantime.
1000677822.korean: 그러던 도중 2차 연합 전쟁이 터지지만 않았더라면 참 좋았을 텐데.

1000677825.english: When the war broke out, the airport was closed... and the port was blockaded.
1000677825.korean: 전쟁이 벌어지자, 공항은 폐쇄되고... 항구가 봉쇄되었습니다.

1000677826.english: I was an important asset to the company, so I had no choice but to stay at Bismarck's UK branch.
1000677826.korean: 저는 회사의 중요한 자산이었기에, 비스마르크 사의 영국 지부에 머물 수 밖에 없었죠.

1000677827.english: While that long and terrible time was passing... the Iron Bug appeared.
1000677827.korean: 그렇게 길고 끔찍한 시간이 흘러가던 도중... 철충이 나타났고요.

1000677828.english: Did you organize a resistance group after that?

1000677830.english: However, the number of Iron Bugs was too great... and we couldn't fight them properly.
1000677830.korean: 하지만, 철충의 수는 너무 많았고... 저희는 철충과 제대로 싸울 수 없었죠.

1000677831.english: To make matters worse, we had no strongholds to defend and were short on supplies.
1000677831.korean: 설상가상으로 저희에겐 방어하기 좋은 거점도 없고, 보급도 모자란 상황이었습니다.

1000677832.english: Naturally, not long after, we were driven into a defensive position.
1000677832.korean: 자연스레, 얼마 지나지 않아 저희는 수세에 몰리게 되었습니다.

1000677833.english: At that time, I received a message from someone calling himself Merlin.
1000677833.korean: 그 때, 자신을 멀린이라고 칭하는 이에게서 연락을 받았습니다.

1000677835.english: There are two dead and five injured.

1000677836.english: ...Understood. Let's move the front line back a little further in the next battle.
1000677836.korean: ...알겠습니다. 다음 전투에서는 전선을 조금 더 뒤로 물리도록 하죠.

1000677837.english: Let's give up Hyde Park and retreat to Buckingham Palace to defend it.
1000677837.korean: 하이드 파크는 포기하고, 버킹엄 궁전까지 후퇴해서 방어하도록 하죠.

1000677838.english: However, then the hospital will come within the range of the shelling.
1000677838.korean: 하지만, 그럼 병원이 포격의 사거리에 들어오게 됩니다.

1000677840.english: I will lure the Iron Bug artillery unit.

1000677841.english: Please just hold the front line until I return.
1000677841.korean: 여러분은 제가 돌아올 때까지 전선을 유지하기만 해 주세요.

1000677842.english: What? Then that means jumping right into the middle of the Iron Bug unit...
1000677842.korean: 네? 그럼, 철충의 부대 한 가운데로 뛰어드는 셈인데...

1000677843.english: It's too dangerous! What if we get hit by concentrated fire...

1000677844.english: Don't worry. I'm strong, so I can handle it.
1000677844.korean: 걱정하지 마세요. 전 튼튼하니까, 버텨낼 수 있어요.

1000677845.english: Besides, I'm the only one who can actively engage the Iron Bug anyway, right?
1000677845.korean: 그리고, 어차피 철충과 적극적으로 교전할 수 있는 것도 저 뿐이잖아요?

1000677846.english: That is true, but... that is too unreasonable a method. We would be better off taking the survivors and evacuating London.
1000677846.korean: 그건 그렇지만... 너무 무리한 방식이에요. 차라리, 생존자들을 데리고 런던에서 철수하죠.

1000677847.english: If Princess were to fall, the resistance itself could collapse.
1000677847.korean: 프린세스 씨가 당하기라도 한다면, 저항군 자체가 와해될 수도 있어요.

1000677848.english: However, if I don't push myself too hard, the resistance will be annihilated.
1000677848.korean: 하지만 제가 무리하지 않는다면 저항군이 전멸하게 되겠죠.

1000677850.english: Judging by the conversation, I arrived at just the right time!
1000677850.korean: 대화를 들어보니, 내가 마침 딱 좋은 시간에 도착했구만!

1000677852.english: No, this voice... seems a bit different...
1000677852.korean: 아니, 이 목소리는... 좀 다른 것 같은데요...

1000677854.english: Hi, I'm Merlin. I couldn't come here in person due to circumstances, so I asked for your understanding that I sent Pubhead instead.
1000677854.korean: 안녕, 난 멀린이야. 사정 상 여기까지 직접 올 수가 없어서, 펍헤드를 대신 보낸 점 양해 부탁할게.

1000677855.english: From what I've heard, it sounds like you guys are in a pretty dangerous situation...
1000677855.korean: 대충 들어보니까, 너희 꽤나 위험한 상황인 것 같은데...

1000677856.english: I brought a way to overcome that crisis situation.
1000677856.korean: 그런 위기 상황을 타파할 방법을 가져왔어.

1000677858.english: It's moving to where I am.
1000677858.korean: 내가 있는 곳으로 옮겨 오는 거야.

1000677859.english: It is the place called Camelot.

1000677860.english: Merlin? I've never heard that name before.

1000677861.english: That's a unique name. Are you part of the Bermuda team?

1000677862.english: ...No, I haven't seen a name like that in the data I have either.
1000677862.korean: ...아니, 내가 가지고 있는 데이터에서도 그런 이름은 못 봤어.

1000677864.english: Oh, sorry for interrupting. Could you continue?
1000677864.korean: 아, 말 끊어서 미안해. 계속 얘기해 줄래?

1000677865.english: There is nothing to be sorry about. It was nice to be able to clear my throat for a moment, too.
1000677865.korean: 미안할 것이 뭐 있나요. 저도 잠깐 목을 가다듬을 수 있어서 좋았어요.

1000677866.english: Anyway... Merlin offered to help us, but asked us to help defend his location in return.
1000677866.korean: 아무튼... 멀린은 저희를 도와주겠다고 제안했고, 대신 자신이 있는 곳을 방어하는 것을 도와달라고 하더군요.

1000677867.english: We could not refuse the offer to provide a base and supplies. We moved to Portsmouth and continued our resistance activities based in Camelot.
1000677867.korean: 거점과 물자를 제공해주겠다는 말을 거절할 수는 없었습니다. 저희는 포츠머스로 이동해, 카멜롯을 기점으로 저항 활동을 계속했습니다.

1000677868.english: Merlin had outstanding strategic insight, so he helped me a lot by my side.
1000677868.korean: 멀린은 뛰어난 전략적 식견을 가지고 있었기에, 제 옆에서 절 많이 도와줬지요.

1000677869.english: Thanks to you, we were able to become inseparable friends.
1000677869.korean: 덕분에, 저희는 둘도 없는 친구가 될 수 있었습니다.

1000677870.english: However... that friendship didn't last long.
1000677870.korean: 하지만... 그 우정은 오래 가지 못했지요.

1000677871.english: Did you lose a friend to an Iron Bug?

1000677872.english: ...No. It met an even more terrible end than that.

1000677873.english: One day, as always... it was after burying the bodies of our resistance comrades.
1000677873.korean: 어느 날, 언제나 그렇듯... 저항군 동료분들의 시신을 매장한 뒤였습니다.

1000677874.english: Leprechaun, you have arrived at just the right time. The result of this battle is...
1000677874.korean: 레프리콘, 마침 잘 왔습니다. 이번 전투 결과는...

1000677878.english: What can I do for you?

1000677879.english: Mr. Merlin said he had something to say and asked everyone to gather.
1000677879.korean: 멀린 씨가 할 말이 있다고, 다들 모여달라고 하시더라고요.

1000677881.english: What a mess... Understood. Where should we meet?
1000677881.korean: 무슨 일이람... 알겠습니다. 어디로 모이면 되죠?

1000677883.english: I have something to tell you all today, so I have gathered you all here.
1000677883.korean: 오늘은 너희한테 할 말이 있어서 이렇게 불러모으게 되었어.

1000677885.english: Ah, the Captain arrived at just the right time.
1000677885.korean: 아, 마침 대장님도 타이밍 좋게 딱 오셨구만.

1000677886.english: I just wanted to say thank you for fighting so hard all this time.
1000677886.korean: 다름이 아니고... 그 동안 열심히 잘 싸워줘서 고맙다고 말하고 싶었어.

1000677887.english: Thanks to that, we were able to reduce the number of Iron Bug near Camelot by about 20%.
1000677887.korean: 덕분에 카멜롯 근처에 있는 철충의 수를 20% 가량 줄일 수 있었거든.

1000677888.english: ...Even though I worked so hard, is it 20%?

1000677889.english: That figure exceeded my expectations, so don't be too disappointed.
1000677889.korean: 그 정도면 내 예상을 뛰어넘은 수치니까, 너무 그렇게 아쉬워 하지 말라고.

1000677890.english: Anyway, I wanted to tell you that thanks to you, the situation has changed a lot.
1000677890.korean: 아무튼, 덕분에 상황이 많이 달라졌다는 걸 말해주고 싶었어.

1000677891.english: Thanks to you guys completely destroying the surrounding Iron Bug production lines, we can now stop the Iron Bug with just Camelot's defenses.
1000677891.korean: 너희가 주변의 철충 생산 라인을 싹 박살낸 덕분에, 이제부터 카멜롯의 방어시설만으로도 철충을 막아낼 수 있게 되었거든.

1000677892.english: So... is there no need to fight anymore?
1000677892.korean: 그럼... 이제 더 싸울 필요가 없는 건가요?

1000677893.english: That's right. It's all thanks to you guys.

1000677895.english: Then there’s no need to keep you guys around, is there?
1000677895.korean: 그럼 굳이 너희를 데리고 있을 필요가 없잖아?

1000677897.english: No, you know. It takes resources to operate Camelot, too...
1000677897.korean: 아니, 그렇잖아. 카멜롯을 가동하는 데에도 자원이 들어가는데...

1000677898.english: Is there really a need to share limited resources with you guys?
1000677898.korean: 한정된 자원을 굳이 너희랑 나눠 쓸 필요가 있을까?

1000677901.english: We risked our lives fighting to defend this place, and now you're saying you'll betray us?
1000677901.korean: 우리는 여길 지키기 위해 목숨을 걸고 싸웠는데, 이제 와서 배신하겠다는 건가요?

1000677902.english: No, why would you express it like that?
1000677902.korean: 아니, 뭐 그걸 그렇게 표현하고 그래.

1000677903.english: "Betrayal" is too strong a word, so I'd like you to be careful.
1000677903.korean: 배신은 너무 강한 말이니까, 좀 주의해줬으면 좋겠어.

1000677904.english: I want to be with you guys too. We’ve grown so attached to each other, and aren’t we comrades who fought together for years? But...
1000677904.korean: 나도 너희랑 같이 있고 싶지. 정도 들대로 다 들었고, 몇 년 동안이나 같이 싸운 전우 아니겠어? 근데...

1000677905.english: My top priority is protecting Camelot.
1000677905.korean: 내 최우선 목표는 카멜롯을 지키는 거거든.

1000677906.english: It's not that I dislike you guys, but I did the math and found this to be the more reasonable choice.
1000677906.korean: 너희가 싫은 건 아닌데, 내가 계산을 해 본 결과 이게 더 합리적인 선택이라서 말이야.

1000677907.english: Wouldn't it be better to save the people who are meant to live?
1000677907.korean: 살 사람은 살리는 편이 좋지 않겠어?

1000677909.english: Hey, let's not get too emotional.

1000677910.english: Black Friday, I don't want to fight with you either, you know? It's quite a headache if you start running wild...
1000677910.korean: 나도 블프, 너랑 싸우고 싶진 않거든? 네가 날뛰기 시작하면 상당히 골치 아프기도 하고...

1000677911.english: I didn't want to see my friend's blood.

1000677914.english: Right, while you were talking, I gathered up every last ounce of security forces. I'm saying you are completely surrounded.
1000677914.korean: 그래, 말하는 사이에 경비 병력을 싸그리 긁어모았지. 너희는 완전히 포위되었단 말씀.

1000677915.english: Let's part ways amicably, my friend.

1000677916.english: After doing something like this, how can you even call yourself a friend?
1000677916.korean: 이런 짓을 해 놓고, 친구라는 말이 입 밖으로 나오나요?

1000677918.english: ...Well, you might think that way right now, but...

1000677919.english: This is all a choice for your sake.

1000677920.english: What do you mean? You're driving us into the jaws of death!
1000677920.korean: 그게 무슨 말이죠? 우리를 사지로 밀어 넣는 중이면서!

1000677921.english: If I go into detail, it would be a spoiler, so please just keep this in mind for now.
1000677921.korean: 자세한 걸 얘기하면 스포일러가 되니까, 지금은 이 정도만 알아 두도록 하세용.

1000677922.english: Anyway, it's really time to leave now! It's a bit of a hassle to leave the guards unattended for too long.
1000677922.korean: 아무튼, 이제 진짜 떠날 시간이야! 경비 병력을 너무 오래 비워놓긴 좀 그렇거든.

1000677923.english: Bye! Adios! Adieu! Have a great day!
1000677923.korean: 안녕! Adios! Adieu! 좋은 하루 보내!

1000677924.english: We won't see each other again!

1000677926.english: Weren't they just used as cannon fodder?

1000677927.english: Merlin controlled Camelot's security AGS units to drive us out of the city. He even set up barricades around us.
1000677927.korean: 멀린은 카멜롯의 경비 AGS들을 조종해 저희를 도시 밖으로 내쫓았습니다. 그리고, 주위에 바리케이드까지 설치해 버리더군요.

1000677928.english: I gathered the remaining people and returned to London. There were still some supplies left there.
1000677928.korean: 저는 남은 이들을 모아 런던으로 다시 돌아갔습니다. 런던에는 물자가 좀 남아있었거든요.

1000677931.english: Yes, it's canned bread. ...the kind they handed out at my fan signing events.
1000677931.korean: 네, 빵 통조림이에요. ...제 팬 사인회 때 나눠주던.

1000677932.english: ...What canned food did you hand out?

1000677934.english: That bizarre thing didn't even appear in Dragon Slayer and My Adventures, so why on earth would they use something like this as a merchandise item for an autograph session...
1000677934.korean: 그런 괴상한 건 용살자와 내 모험에는 등장하지도 않았던 물건인데, 대체 왜 이런 걸 사인회의 상품으로...

1000677935.english: No, that's not right. It wouldn't make sense to say it now.
1000677935.korean: 아니, 아니다. 지금 말해봐야 의미도 없는 일이지.

1000677936.english: Sigh, anyway, how long has it been since you were kicked out like that?
1000677936.korean: 에휴, 그나저나, 그렇게 쫓겨난 게 얼마나 되었느냐?

1000677937.english: It hasn't been that long. It's been about one to two weeks.
1000677937.korean: 그렇게 오래 되지는 않았어요. 약 일주일에서 이주일 정도입니다.

1000677938.english: It really hasn't been that long. Moving to London must have been quite tight, too.
1000677938.korean: 정말 얼마 되지 않았군. 런던으로 옮겨오는 것도 꽤나 촉박했겠어.

1000677939.english: But did they even send a distress signal in the midst of such a desperate situation? That's impressive.
1000677939.korean: 그런데, 그런 급박한 와중에 구조 신호까지 보낸 건가? 대단하군.

1000677941.english: I've never sent anything like that.

1000677942.english: Huh? Didn't you send a distress signal in your name?
1000677942.korean: 응? 당신 이름으로 구조 신호를 발신하지 않았어?

1000677944.english: Hmm, did one of the survivors send this...?
1000677944.korean: 으음, 생존자 분들 중 한 분이 보내셨나...?

1000677945.english: Then, did you come to help us, rather than to join the resistance?
1000677945.korean: 그럼 여러분은 저항군에 합류하러 오신 게 아니라, 저희를 도우러 오신 분들인가요?

1000677946.english: Is there another group of survivors in the UK?
1000677946.korean: 영국에 또 다른 생존자 집단이 있는 건가요?

1000677947.english: No. We came through France. Through the undersea tunnel.
1000677947.korean: 아니. 우리는 프랑스를 통해서 왔어. 해저 터널로 말이야.

1000677949.english: To think you crossed the sea to save us.
1000677949.korean: 저희를 구하기 위해, 바다까지 넘어 오시다니.

1000677951.english: Hmph, as the saying goes, 'Noblesse Oblige,' shouldn't a princess of the True Ancestors, who is nobler than anyone else, naturally extend a helping hand to the weak?
1000677951.korean: 훗, 노블리스 오블리주란 말도 있듯이, 그 누구보다도 고귀한 진조의 프린세스라면 마땅히 약자들에게 도움의 손길을 베풀어야 하지 않겠느냐.

1000677952.english: ...Hehe, you're right. I think that's correct.
1000677952.korean: ...후후, 그러네요. 그 말이 맞는 것 같아요.

1000677953.english: Then, who is leading the survivor group outside?
1000677953.korean: 그럼, 바깥의 생존자 집단은 누가 이끌고 있나요?

1000677954.english: Hmm... I guess there will be a lot to explain.

1000677955.english: First of all, I need to explain about the Last Human, the reincarnation of the Dragon Slayer...
1000677955.korean: 일단, 용살자의 환생인 최후의 인간에 대해서부터 설명을 해야겠는데...

1000677958.english: It was a distress signal sent to the Blind Princess's account, but... she never said she was the Blind Princess in the signal.
1000677958.korean: 블라인드 프린세스의 계정으로 보내진 구조신호긴 하지만... 신호 내에서 자기가 블라인드 프린세스라고 말한 적은 없었으니까.

1000677959.english: So, it's possible that one of the survivors sent a signal amidst the chaos.
1000677959.korean: 그러니, 혼란을 틈타 생존자들 중 한 명이 신호를 보냈을 가능성도 있겠지.

1000677960.english: Isn't there a possibility that they lied?

1000677961.english: That's right. But what benefit would there be in lying in this situation?
1000677961.korean: 그렇지. 하지만 이 상황에서 거짓말을 해서 얻을 이득이 뭐가 있겠어?

1000677963.english: Still, something is weighing on my mind...

1000677964.english: There is one more thing I’d like to hear.
1000677964.korean: 한 가지 더 듣고 싶은 게 있는데 말이야.

1000677967.english: Oh no, it's an Iron Bug attack.

1000677968.english: will go defeat the Iron Bug. Everyone, please take refuge in a safe place!
1000677968.korean: 저는 철충을 격퇴하러 가겠습니다. 여러분은 안전한 곳에 피신해 계세요!

1000677969.english: What nonsense! I will fight alongside you.

1000677970.english: As I said, those of noble lineage ought to offer help.
1000677970.korean: 말했듯이, 고귀한 혈통을 가진 이는 마땅히 도움을 베풀어야 하니까.

1000677971.english: Yeah, we're used to fighting Iron Bug too. You could say we're experts in this field.
1000677971.korean: 그래, 우리도 철충과 싸우는 건 익숙하거든. 이 분야의 전문가라고 할 수 있지.

1000677972.english: ...In that case, I humbly ask... for your help.

1000677974.english: It seems we've defeated all the Metal Parasites that attacked the defensive line.
1000677974.korean: 방어선을 공격한 철충은 다 무찌른 것 같네.

1000677975.english: But, something's been bothering me since earlier…
1000677975.korean: 그런데, 아까부터 마음 한 켠에 좀 걸리는 게 있는데...

1000677977.english: Aside from the fact that their attacks were unusually strong?
1000677977.korean: 놈들의 공격이 이상할 정도로 거세다는 것을 제외하고 말이냐?

1000677978.english: Yeah. Besides that, the Metal Parasites' movements were strange.
1000677978.korean: 응. 그거 외에도, 철충들의 움직임이 이상했어.

1000677979.english: They were trying to avoid direct collisions with us and move around the sides. Am I imagining it?
1000677979.korean: 우리와 정면으로 충돌하는 걸 피해서, 옆으로 돌아가려고 하던데. 내 착각인가?

1000677980.english: No, you're not. I noticed it too.

1000677981.english: From what I confirmed, they seemed to be trying to detour toward the place where the survivors are gathered.
1000677981.korean: 확인한 바로는, 생존자들이 모여 있는 곳으로 우회하려고 시도한 것 같습니다.

1000677982.english: What…! Are the survivors okay? Is anyone injured…
1000677982.korean: 그런...! 생존자 분들은 괜찮으신가요? 다친 사람이 있다면...

1000677983.english: Don't worry. We placed capable operatives at the choke points to block them.
1000677983.korean: 걱정하지 마세요. 길목에 실력이 확실한 대원을 배치해서 차단했으니까.

1000677984.english: ...Wait, didn't you tell me to stand over there earlier?
1000677984.korean: ...잠깐, 아까 나보고 저기 서있으라고 하지 않았어?

1000677985.english: I had a feeling a lot of them were coming.

1000677986.english: Don't get too upset. It's actually a very good way to block the Metal Parasites' approach.
1000677986.korean: 너무 화내지 마라. 철충의 접근을 차단하기에는 아주 좋은 방법이 아닌가.

1000677987.english: Really? Then how about you block the path next time? Invisible obstacles can block enemies as effectively as burning barriers.
1000677987.korean: 그래? 그럼 다음엔 네가 길을 막는 건 어때? 불타는 장애물만큼이나 안 보이는 장애물도 적을 잘 막을 텐데.

1000677988.english: Uh, um… that's not what I meant…
1000677988.korean: 어, 음... 내 말은 그런 뜻이 아니라...

1000677989.english: …Ah, right, I forgot to go check if the Metal Parasites have really stopped moving.
1000677989.korean: ...아, 아차, 철충이 확실히 움직임을 멈췄는지 확인하러 가야 하는 걸 잊고 있었다.

1000677990.english: Then I'll leave it at that…

1000677991.english: Ahaha. You two get along well.

1000677992.english: By the way, the Metal Parasites' movements are surprisingly tactical. They're holding us here and trying to attack survivors who have no resistance.
1000677992.korean: 그나저나 철충의 움직임이 상당히 전술적인데? 우리를 여기 잡아두고, 저항 능력이 없는 생존자들을 공격하려고 하다니.

1000677993.english: Ah, that… is because of the recent Connector. I was going to mention it anyway.
1000677993.korean: 아, 그건... 최근 나타난 연결체 때문입니다. 안 그래도 말씀을 드리려 했는데.

1000677995.english: Yes. I couldn't see what it looked like, but…
1000677995.korean: 예. 어떻게 생겼는지는 볼 수 없었지만...

1000677996.english: It was extremely intelligent and fully leveraged the advantage of its overwhelming numbers to pressure us.
1000677996.korean: 매우 지능적이었고, 엄청난 물량에서 오는 이점을 십분 발휘해 저희를 압박했습니다.

1000677997.english: Because of it, even with Camelot on our side, defending against the Metal Parasites' attacks was very difficult.
1000677997.korean: 녀석 때문에 카멜롯을 끼고 있음에도 불구하고 철충의 공격을 방어하기가 매우 힘들었죠.

1000677998.english: A Connector that not only actively hunts Bioroids but is also cunning…
1000677998.korean: 적극적으로 바이오로이드를 사냥하는 공격성을 갖춘데다, 교활함까지 갖춘 연결체라...

1000677999.english: It may sound strange to say this, but it's fortunate that it was trapped in England.
1000677999.korean: 이렇게 말씀드리긴 좀 그렇지만, 놈이 영국에 갇혀 있던 게 다행이군요.

1000678000.english: Yes. For us, it was unlucky though.

1000678001.english: Such a troublesome enemy… I'll have to tell Watson later.
1000678001.korean: 그런 골치 아픈 적이라니... 나중에 왓슨한테 알려줘야겠다.

1000678002.english: I'll make a record of it.

1000678003.english: …As expected, I want to bring it to City Guard.

1000678004.english: Anyway, with such a dangerous enemy, we can't stay in London.
1000678004.korean: 아무튼, 이렇게 위험한 적이 있는데 계속 런던에 머물 수는 없어.

1000678005.english: We set up barricades here and there, but the terrain isn't ideal for defense…
1000678005.korean: 바리케이드를 여기저기 설치해두긴 했지만, 그래도 방어하기 좋은 지형도 아니고...

1000678006.english: Supplies are running low, and it's hard to hold out by intercepting enemies in advance like this.
1000678006.korean: 남은 물자도 얼마 없어서, 지금처럼 미리 적을 요격하면서 버티기도 힘들어.

1000678007.english: Are you thinking of retreating immediately?

1000678008.english: Yes, I think we need to retreat right away with the survivors.
1000678008.korean: 응, 생존자들을 데리고 바로 후퇴해야 할 것 같아.

1000678009.english: It should still be okay though, right?

1000678010.english: Yes. Everyone is prepared to evacuate at any moment.
1000678010.korean: 예. 다들 언제든지 대피할 준비를 마쳐 둔 상태입니다.

1000678012.english: Since we don't know when the next attack will come, it's better to leave quickly.
1000678012.korean: 언제 다음 공격이 올지 모르는 상황이니까, 빨리 빠져나가는 편이 좋겠어.

1000678015.english: I have a favor to ask.

1000678016.english: In my opinion, the enemy we need to be most careful of is the enemy Connector.
1000678016.korean: 내 생각에, 우리가 가장 주의해야 할 적은 적 연결체야.

1000678017.english: So, if the Connector pursues us and a battle breaks out…
1000678017.korean: 그러니, 만약 연결체가 우리를 추격해 와서, 전투가 벌어진다면...

1000678018.english: Phantom, stay out of the fight and search for the Connector. Got it?
1000678018.korean: 팬텀 씨는 전투에서 빠져서 연결체를 수색해 줘. 알았지?

1000678019.english: Then we'll have one less fighter…
1000678019.korean: 그럼 전투 인원이 한 명 줄어드는 셈인데...

1000678020.english: It'll be fine. Brünhilde is here, and so are the Saint and the Princess. Aias also fights well.
1000678020.korean: 괜찮을 거야. 브륀힐드 씨도 있고, 성녀님이랑 공주님도 있으니까. 아이아스도 잘 싸우고.

1000678021.english: What matters in this fight is not killing a lot of Metal Parasites, but quickly taking out the enemy Connector.
1000678021.korean: 이 싸움에서 중요한 건 철충을 많이 잡는 게 아니라, 적 연결체를 빠르게 끊어내는 거야.

1000678022.english: The command system is concentrated in that one being, which means if we eliminate it, the enemy will collapse immediately.
1000678022.korean: 지휘체계가 한 놈에게 몰려 있다는 건, 그 녀석만 처리하면 바로 적군이 붕괴한다는 뜻이기도 하거든.

1000678023.english: You mean hit the head quickly.

1000678024.english: Yes. I don't think we'd lose playing chess against it…
1000678024.korean: 응. 녀석이랑 체스를 둔다고 해서 질 것 같진 않지만...

1000678025.english: But I don't want to fairly face a coward using forty-two pieces by himself.
1000678025.korean: 혼자서 기물을 마흔 두 개 정도 쓰는 비겁한 놈이랑 정정당당하게 붙어주긴 싫단 말이지.

1000678028.english: I'll make the assassination of the Connector our top priority.
1000678028.korean: 연결체 암살을 최우선 과제로 놓도록 하겠다.

1000678029.english: Good. Then, with insurance in place…

1000678030.english: Shall we return to the Orca?

1000678031.english: These Iron Bugs are really persistent!

1000678032.english: No matter how much I catch, there is no end to it!

1000678033.english: Haven't you arrived yet! The Black River Base is right in front of you!
1000678033.korean: 그래도 도착하지 않았느냐! 바로 코 앞이 블랙리버 기지이니라!

1000678034.english: I will go in and block the entrance right away!

1000678035.english: You all worked hard! We’ve made it this far without any casualties, so let’s keep going like that until the end!
1000678035.korean: 다들 수고 많았어! 그래도 아직까진 사상자 없이 왔으니까, 끝까지 그렇게 가자!

1000678036.english: While we hold off the Iron Bug at the entrance, let the non-combatants get into the tunnel first...
1000678036.korean: 우리가 입구에서 철충을 막는 동안, 일단 비전투원부터 터널 안쪽으로 들여보내고...

1000678037.english: Wait, over there! On the hill!

1000678039.english: It's shelling! Everyone, get out of the way!

1000678041.english: (Based on the direction and time from which the shelling sound was heard, the timing to intercept the shell is...)
1000678041.korean: (포격음이 들려온 방향과 시간을 통해 계산했을 때, 포탄을 요격할 타이밍은...)

1000678043.english: Mr. Aias! Please raise your shield!

1000678047.english: (Unexpectedly... the strength of the shield is strong!)

1000678048.english: We dealt with the shells falling toward us.

1000678049.english: ...Stepped on a shield, jumped into the air... then headbutted a cannonball and made it explode?
1000678049.korean: ...방패를 밟고 공중으로 뛰어오른 다음... 포탄에 박치기를 날려서 폭발시켰다고?

1000678050.english: Please save the praise for later. The situation is urgent right now.
1000678050.korean: 칭찬은 나중으로 미뤄주세요. 지금은 급박한 상황이니까.

1000678051.english: What kind of compliment is that!

1000678052.english: Wait, is that greatsword you're holding just for show? Couldn't you have sliced through a cannonball with it, delivered an elegant kick, or at least used your fists?!
1000678052.korean: 아니, 손에 들고 있는 그 대검은 장식이냐? 검으로 포탄을 베어버리거나, 우아하게 발차기를 날리거나, 하다못해 주먹을 쓸 수는 없던 게냐?!

1000678054.english: A headbutt, my God, a headbutt...!

1000678055.english: And yet you call yourself a saint...?! Where did you throw your dignity and nobility?
1000678055.korean: 그러고도 성녀라고 자칭할 수 있느냐...?! 품위와 고상함은 어디에 가져다 버리고?

1000678056.english: Of course she can be called a saint; after all, she protected her allies.
1000678056.korean: 당연히 성녀라고 할 수 있죠, 어쨌거나 아군을 지켰잖아요.

1000678057.english: Are you okay? You didn't get hurt?!

1000678062.english: The Iron Bug has formed a siege right before my eyes, and the escape route has collapsed... I'm completely trapped.
1000678062.korean: 눈 앞에는 철충이 포위망을 만들었고, 탈출구는 무너졌으니... 완전히 갇혀버렸네.

1000678063.english: never imagined I would die here.
1000678063.korean: 여기서 죽을 거라고는 상상도 못 했는데.

1000678064.english: It's too early to give up.

1000678066.english: As I mentioned before, I looked for the enemy connection...
1000678066.korean: 전에 말했던 대로, 적 연결체를 찾아봤는데...

1000678067.english: The connection was not visible. Long-distance communication was impossible for the enemies as well. In other words...
1000678067.korean: 연결체는 보이지 않았다. 원거리 통신이 불가능한 건 적들도 마찬가지. 즉...

1000678068.english: So you're saying the connector isn't here.

1000678069.english: In that case, this isn't a raid commanded by the Nexus...
1000678069.korean: 그렇다면 이건 연결체가 지휘하는 습격이 아니라...

1000678070.english: It is highly likely that the Iron Bug's guards have spotted us and are attacking.
1000678070.korean: 철충들의 경계병력이 저희를 발견하고 공격해 오는 것일 가능성이 크겠군요.

1000678071.english: Right. Then the enemies won't be able to show an immediate response to our actions.
1000678071.korean: 그래. 그럼 적들은 우리 행동에 맞춰서 즉각적인 대처를 보이진 못할 거야.

1000678073.english: Where was the thinnest part of the encirclement?

1000678075.english: ...There are no other options. For now, let's go back to London and think about what to do next.
1000678075.korean: ...다른 선택지가 없네. 일단, 런던으로 돌아가서 다음 일을 생각해보자.

1000678076.english: Everyone, follow me! We're going back to London!
1000678076.korean: 다들, 잘 따라와! 런던으로 돌아갈 거야!

1000678077.english: Phew… really, there are too many Metal Parasites.

1000678078.english: And there are more than a few people to take care of. They're in the way, really.
1000678078.korean: 그런 와중에 챙길 사람은 한둘이 아니고. 걸리적거린단 말이지, 정말.

1000678079.english: That's not something someone protecting people from the rear would say.
1000678079.korean: 맨 뒤에서 사람들을 보호하면서 온 사람이 할 법한 말은 아니구나.

1000678080.english: Don't misunderstand. I just didn't feel like walking quickly.
1000678080.korean: 오해하지 마. 그냥 빨리 걷기가 싫었을 뿐이니까.

1000678081.english: Hehe. Of course. I know that.
1000678081.korean: 후후. 물론 그렇겠지. 짐도 다 안다.

1000678083.english: No one was left behind, right?

1000678084.english: Yes, thanks to everyone, fortunately, no one was injured, and we arrived in London safely.
1000678084.korean: 예, 여러분 덕분에, 다행히 아무도 다치지 않고 런던에 도착할 수 있었습니다.

1000678085.english: …I'm not sure if it's really fortunate though.

1000678086.korean: 남은 물자가 거의 바닥을 보이고 있는 상황입니다.

1000678087.english: We can't stay in London any longer.
1000678087.korean: 더 이상 런던에서 머무를 수는 없어요.

1000678089.english: Exactly. With the Connector knowing our every move, it's troublesome in many ways.
1000678089.korean: 그러게나 말이다. 연결체가 우리 일거수 일투족을 다 알고 있는 상황이니 여러모로 곤란하구나.

1000678091.english: Hm? What do you mean it's not?

1000678092.english: We kept scanning the surroundings, but there were no Metal Parasites watching our movements.
1000678092.korean: 주위를 계속 살폈지만, 우리 움직임을 감시하는 철충은 없다.

1000678093.english: So, the Connector isn't moving based on knowledge of our movements.
1000678093.korean: 그러니, 연결체가 우리의 움직임을 알고서 움직이는 건 아니다.

1000678094.english: Then how did it know we would go to the tunnel?
1000678094.korean: 그렇다면 어떻게 우리가 터널로 갈 거라고 알았던 거지?

1000678095.english: In my opinion, it didn't know our movements.
1000678095.korean: 내 생각에, 그건 우리 움직임을 알고 있던 게 아니야.

1000678096.english: It divided the Metal Parasites into several units and sent them in advance to places we were likely to go. If it had known we would go to the tunnel, it would have come directly to the tunnel itself.
1000678096.korean: 철충을 여러 부대로 나눠서, 우리가 갈 법한 곳으로 미리 보내두었던 거지. 만약 우리가 터널로 갈 거라는 사실을 알았다면, 직접 터널로 왔을 거야.

1000678097.english: Then that's a relief. It means our movements aren't being watched.
1000678097.korean: 그렇다면 다행이구나. 우리 움직임이 감시당하고 있지는 않다는 뜻이니까.

1000678099.english: Knowing the places we're likely to go means it understands our behavior and can plan schemes accordingly, right?
1000678099.korean: 우리가 갈 법한 곳을 알고 있다는 건, 우리 행동 방식을 파악하고 그에 맞춰서 계략을 짤 수 있다는 뜻이잖아?

1000678100.english: I thought it was just a cunning enemy, but it seems to be a bit more dangerous.
1000678100.korean: 녀석이 그냥 교활한 적이라고 생각했는데, 아무래도 좀 더 위험한 녀석인 것 같아.

1000678101.english: We should probably assign it a codename for distinction.
1000678101.korean: 아무래도 코드네임을 붙여 구분을 해 둘 필요가 있을 것 같습니다.

1000678102.english: Later, we'll need to upload information on this Metal Parasite to the Orca's database.
1000678102.korean: 추후 오르카의 데이터베이스에 해당 철충의 정보를 업로드 할 필요가 있으니까요.

1000678103.english: Hmm, then… what should we call it?

1000678104.english: It's a special entity that appeared only in England…

1000678108.english: Sherlock is Japanese, but Sherlock isn't a Japanese name!
1000678108.korean: 셜록은 일본인인데, 셜록은 일본인 이름이 아니잖아!

1000678110.english: Well… it's not really a name, it's more like a nickname.
1000678110.korean: 그건 그러니까... 이름이 아니라 별명 같은 거다.

1000678111.english: It's named after the famous detective Sherlock Holmes from British detective novels because it always uncovers the truth.
1000678111.korean: 영국의 추리소설에 나오는 명탐정 셜록 홈즈처럼, 언제나 진실을 찾아내기 때문에 붙은 이름이지.

1000678114.english: Ahem, anyway… seeing you bring up that name, it's clear you know nothing about Sherlock Holmes.
1000678114.korean: 흠흠, 아무튼... 그 이름을 꺼내는 걸 보니 셜록 홈즈에 대해 아무것도 모르는구만.

1000678115.english: Yeah, I know nothing! So Sherlock, tell me!
1000678115.korean: 응, 아무것도 몰라! 그러니까 셜록이 알려줘!

1000678116.english: Well, since there's nothing else to do, why not?
1000678116.korean: 뭐, 마침 할 일도 없었는데 그럴까?

1000678119.english: …That's how it went. Now you should understand how amazing Sherlock Holmes is as a detective.
1000678119.korean: ...이랬던 거지. 이제 셜록 홈즈가 얼마나 대단한 명탐정인지 알겠지?

1000678120.english: …I get it, Sherlock really really likes Sherlock Holmes.
1000678120.korean: ...셜록이 셜록 홈즈를 정말정말 좋아한다는 건 잘 알겠어.

1000678121.english: Said "briefly," but talked nonstop about the novels for an hour.
1000678121.korean: 짧게 설명한다고 하고, 1시간동안 쉬지 않고 소설 얘기를 할 줄이야.

1000678123.english: So, who's the worst guy in Sherlock Holmes?
1000678123.korean: 그래서, 셜록 홈즈에서 제일 나쁜 놈은 누구야?

1000678125.english: Every time Sherlock says something bad, I'll call him by that name! Hehe.
1000678125.korean: 셜록이 나쁜 말 할 때마다 그 이름으로 부를 거야! 히히.

1000678127.english: Well, to answer that… it has to be Moriarty. A criminal with a genius-level mind.
1000678127.korean: 뭐, 그 질문에 대답하자면... 역시 모리아티겠지. 천재적인 두뇌를 지닌 범죄자.

1000678128.english: But even he eventually knelt before Sherlock Holmes.
1000678128.korean: 하지만 그 놈도 결국에는 셜록 홈즈 앞에 무릎을 꿇었다는 말씀.

1000678129.english: Which means the truth will eventually be revealed!
1000678129.korean: 그러니까, 진실은 결국 언젠가 밝혀지기 마련이란 말이야!

1000678130.english: Alright! Let's cheer up and investigate another case!
1000678130.korean: 좋아! 다시 기운 내서 다른 사건 조사하러 가자!

1000678134.english: A cunning enemy, but he ultimately kneels before Watson and Holmes.
1000678134.korean: 교활한 적이지만, 결국 왓슨과 홈즈 앞에 무릎을 꿇으니까 말이야.

1000678135.english: That's a reasonable reason. Then we'll record the Connector's codename as Moriarty.
1000678135.korean: 괜찮은 이유네요. 그럼 연결체의 코드네임은 모리아티라고 기록하겠습니다.

1000678136.english: Then… what now? If we don't deal with the Connector, it will keep targeting us.
1000678136.korean: 그럼... 이제 어떻게 하죠? 연결체를 처리하지 않으면, 놈은 계속 저희를 노릴 겁니다.

1000678137.english: But we don't really have a clever way to handle it, do we?
1000678137.korean: 그렇다고 해서 뾰족한 수가 있는 것도 아니잖아?

1000678138.english: If it keeps hiding like now and only sends its subordinates, we won't be able to do anything.
1000678138.korean: 지금처럼 모습을 감추고 부하들만 계속 보내면, 우리는 아무 것도 못 할 걸.

1000678139.english: Hmm, because of the ambush in the tunnel earlier, some people were injured and the number of combat-ready personnel decreased…
1000678139.korean: 으음, 아까 터널에서 습격을 당한 탓에, 부상자가 생겨 전투 인원이 더 줄어들었는데...

1000678140.english: How many are left capable of fighting?
1000678140.korean: 현재 전투가 가능한 인원이 얼마나 남았느냐?

1000678142.english: So you're saying six people have to face all the Metal Parasites…
1000678142.korean: 여섯 명이서 철충 전체를 상대해야 한다는 얘긴가...

1000678144.english: We have to contact the Orca.

1000678145.english: …That would be the best way, but… that's impossible.

1000678146.english: With our current equipment, we can't break through the jamming signals.
1000678146.korean: 현재 가진 장비로는 방해전파를 뚫을 수 없다.

1000678147.english: No, I've thought of a way.

1000678150.english: Considering that the place that sent the signal to us in the first place was Camelot...
1000678150.korean: 애초에 저희에게 신호를 보낸 곳이 카멜롯이라는 점을 생각했을 때...

1000678151.english: The point is that if we use Camelot's communication equipment, we will be able to contact the Orca.
1000678151.korean: 카멜롯의 통신 장비를 이용한다면, 오르카 호에 연락을 할 수 있을 거라는 것이죠.

1000678152.english: Rather than breaking through the massive army of Iron Bug and assassinating the Nexus, it would be a better option to launch a surprise attack on Camelot, capture it, and call in reinforcements.
1000678152.korean: 철충의 대군을 뚫고 연결체를 암살하는 것보다, 카멜롯을 기습공격해서 점령하고 지원군을 부르는 쪽이 더 나은 선택지일 겁니다.

1000678153.english: That's a good idea, but... there are barricades set up around Portsmouth.
1000678153.korean: 좋은 생각이긴 하지만... 포츠머스 주위에는 바리케이드가 설치되어 있어요.

1000678154.english: If there is no way to get over this barricade, the plan will be in vain.
1000678154.korean: 이 바리케이드를 넘을 방법이 없다면, 계획은 무용지물이 되고 말아요.

1000678155.english: There is also a way to do that.

1000678156.english: London Waterloo Station nearby will be the answer.
1000678156.korean: 근처에 있는 런던 워털루 역이 바로 그 해답이 되어줄 겁니다.

1000678157.english: ...Surely not, it's not what I'm thinking, right?

1000678159.english: What are you all talking about?

1000678160.english: Last time, while looking around with Aias, I also investigated London Waterloo Station.
1000678160.korean: 저번에 아이아스랑 같이 주위를 살펴보면서 런던 워털루 역도 조사했거든.

1000678161.english: The train lines from London Waterloo Station mainly go to southern coastal cities.
1000678161.korean: 런던 워털루 역의 기차 노선들은, 주로 남부의 해안도시들로 통하더라고.

1000678165.english: You just need to break through the barricade with a train.

1000678166.english: (I thought this person had a quiet personality...)

1000678168.english: (As expected, everyone would oppose such a radical plan...)

1000678169.english: Hahaha! Good! That is the kind of thing that can be called a princess's procession!
1000678169.korean: 하하하핫! 좋다! 그 정도는 되어야 프린세스의 행차라고 할 수 있지!

1000678173.english: I absolutely love it! That exhilarating feeling! And that refreshing coolness!
1000678173.korean: 마음에 쏙 들어요! 그 호쾌함! 그리고 그 시원함!

1000678176.english: But well, there isn't really any other good option.
1000678176.korean: 하지만 뭐, 다른 뾰족한 수도 없으니까.

1000678178.english: Even though it might seem like a reckless choice at first glance...

1000678179.english: If we add some steel plates to the train and modify the front... I think it would be quite possible.
1000678179.korean: 기차에 철판을 좀 덧대고, 전면부를 개조하면... 충분히 가능할 것 같아.

1000678181.english: (It’s a really terrifying plan, but if even Ms. Leanne says that...)
1000678181.korean: (정말 무시무시한 계획이지만, 리앤 씨까지 저렇게 말한다면야...)

1000678182.english: Understood. I will participate as well.

1000678183.english: Alright. Then, let's sweep the area around the train station clean... and then start reinforcing the train.
1000678183.korean: 좋아. 그럼, 기차역 주위를 싹 쓸어버린 다음... 기차 보강 작업을 시작하자.

1000678184.english: I'm taking the train to Portsmouth and going to Camelot!
1000678184.korean: 포츠머스행 기차를 타고, 카멜롯으로 가는 거야!

1000678187.english: So, what should we do now?

1000678188.english: The train to Portsmouth… yes, there it is.

1000678190.english: Yes. Some of the survivors are familiar with this kind of work…
1000678190.korean: 네. 생존자 분들 중에 이런 작업에 익숙하신 분들이 계시니까...

1000678192.english: Good, good. I like that very much.

1000678193.english: But the more hands, the better, so we'll help with the work too!
1000678193.korean: 하지만 손은 많을수록 좋으니, 우리도 작업을 돕도록 하마!

1000678194.english: Almost done. Now we just need to finish tomorrow morning, right?
1000678194.korean: 거의 다 끝냈다. 이제 내일 아침에 마무리만 하면 되겠는데?

1000678195.english: Go in first. I'll check this area a little more before I go in.
1000678195.korean: 먼저 들어가세요. 전 여기만 더 점검하고 들어갈게요.

1000678196.english: It's dark, and there's not much to see, so how about stopping the work now?
1000678196.korean: 어두워서 보이는 것도 별로 없는데, 작업은 이제 그만 하는게 어때?

1000678197.english: I'm fine. Anyway, I can't really see.

1000678199.english: Hehe. You'll get used to it.

1000678201.english: Alright. Then don't overdo it and come back when you've done enough. We need to finish tomorrow anyway.
1000678201.korean: 알았어. 그럼, 너무 무리하지 말고 적당히 하다가 들어와. 어차피 내일 마무리해야 하니까.

1000678203.english: Phew, as expected… when I tapped the iron plate earlier, it seemed like this screw wasn't tightened properly.
1000678203.korean: 휴, 역시... 아까 철판을 두드려 봤을 때, 이쪽 나사가 제대로 조여지지 않은 것 같더라니.

1000678204.english: If we tighten it like this…

1000678205.english: Tighten it moderately. The screw is getting crushed.

1000678207.english: That voice is yours, Brünhilde, right? We haven't had a proper conversation since you've been so busy.
1000678207.korean: 그 목소리는 브륀힐드 씨지요? 바빠서 제대로 대화를 나눈 적은 없는 것 같네요.

1000678210.english: It seems you came to me with a special matter.
1000678210.korean: 특별한 용건을 가지고 절 찾아오신 것 같은데요.

1000678211.english: I had a few things I wanted to ask.
1000678211.korean: 몇 가지, 묻고 싶은 게 있었거든.

1000678212.english: Reasons for founding the resistance, reasons for leading people…
1000678212.korean: 저항군을 설립한 이유라던가, 사람들을 이끌던 이유라던가...

1000678213.english: Hmm, you were curious about that. Actually, it's not an extraordinary reason.
1000678213.korean: 으음, 그게 궁금하셨군요. 사실 엄청나게 대단한 이유는 아니에요.

1000678216.english: Yes. I… perhaps because I'm blind, my senses are quite sensitive.
1000678216.korean: 예. 전... 눈이 멀어서 그런가, 감각이 꽤 예민한 편이거든요.

1000678217.english: So, when I was ordered by Bismark executives to kill Metal Parasites and fought them… I heard the screams from the surrounding Bioroids.
1000678217.korean: 그래서, 비스마르크 사의 임원분들께 철충을 죽이라는 명령을 받고 철충과 싸우다가... 주변의 바이오로이드들이 내는 비명을 듣게 되었죠.

1000678218.english: …It was horrific. Full of screams and moans. The agony-filled screams of those whose limbs were torn off, the last breath of someone shot in the lungs…
1000678218.korean: ...끔찍했어요. 비명소리와 신음소리가 가득했죠. 팔다리가 날아간 이들의 고통에 찬 비명소리, 허파에 총을 맞은 이가 마지막으로 내쉬는 바람 빠진 숨소리...

1000678219.english: I never want to hear that sound again.

1000678221.english: So, because the sound was so terrifying… I protected them. It wasn't easy, but somehow I managed.
1000678221.korean: 그래서, 그 소리가 너무 무서워서... 그들을 보호했어요. 쉬운 일은 아니었지만, 어떻게든 해낼 수 있었죠.

1000678222.english: Running around frantically to protect everyone, people gradually gathered around me…
1000678222.korean: 모두를 보호하려고 여기저기 정신없이 뛰어다니다 보니, 어느새 사람들은 저를 중심으로 모여들었고...

1000678223.english: That's how the resistance was formed.

1000678227.english: Sometimes, actions don't need that many reasons.
1000678227.korean: 가끔은 행동에 그리 많은 이유가 필요하지 않기도 하지요.

1000678228.english: Didn't the lab director who created Brünhilde also say something like that?
1000678228.korean: 브륀힐드 씨를 만든 연구소장님도 그렇게 말씀하시지 않았나요?

1000678230.english: Yes. Of course, this is the first time we're actually talking.
1000678230.korean: 예. 물론, 직접 이야기를 나눠보는 건 이게 처음이지만요.

1000678231.english: You have quite a sinister side. You've been pretending not to know me all this time?
1000678231.korean: 당신, 상당히 음흉한 구석이 있네. 여태까지 모르는 척하고 있었단 말이야?

1000678232.english: Hehe. It would be weird if I acted friendly first, wouldn't it?
1000678232.korean: 후후. 제 쪽에서 먼저 친한 척 말 거는 것도 이상하잖아요?

1000678233.english: I was secretly waiting for you, Brünhilde, to speak to me first.
1000678233.korean: 브륀힐드 씨가 먼저 말을 걸어주시길, 내심 기다리고 있었죠.

1000678234.english: So, is that a sufficient answer?

1000678236.english: But this isn't the story you really wanted to hear, is it?
1000678236.korean: 하지만, 당신이 듣고 싶던 이야기는 이게 아니지 않나요?

1000678238.english: Well… I'm quite an experienced actress.
1000678238.korean: 뭐... 저는 꽤 경력 있는 여배우니까요.

1000678239.english: I can see through most performances.
1000678239.korean: 어지간한 연기는 꿰뚫어 볼 수 있답니다?

1000678240.english: I can't actually see them, though. Hehe…

1000678242.english: You'll have to get used to it.

1000678244.english: Yes. Actually, what I really wanted to ask was about myself.
1000678244.korean: 그래. 사실 진짜로 물어보고 싶던 건, 내 얘기였어.

1000678245.english: Why did you refuse to film with me?
1000678245.korean: 왜 네가 나랑 영화를 찍는 걸 거절했는지.

1000678247.english: I don't really like killing anyone.
1000678247.korean: 전 누굴 죽이는 걸 그리 좋아하지 않거든요.

1000678248.english: And the lab director also asked me to help keep Brünhilde alive.
1000678248.korean: 그리고, 연구소장님이 브륀힐드 씨를 살릴 수 있게 도와 달라는 부탁도 하셨고요.

1000678250.english: Yes. Originally in the script… Brünhilde died very tragically. She was a role that was consumed without any meaning.
1000678250.korean: 예. 원래 각본에서... 브륀힐드 씨는 매우 비참하게 죽었거든요. 아무런 의미 없이 소모되는 역할이었죠.

1000678251.english: The lab director didn't seem to like that very much.
1000678251.korean: 연구소장님은 그게 별로 마음에 들지 않으셨던 것 같아요.

1000678252.english: He said, "My work is the best, so it should be treated accordingly. Even if she dies, she cannot die like this."
1000678252.korean: 이렇게 말씀하셨어요. ‘내 작품은 최고니까, 그에 맞는 대우를 받아야 한다. 설령 죽더라도, 이렇게 죽일 수는 없다’ 고 말이죠.

1000678253.english: …That really sounds like something she would say.

1000678255.english: Well, if it had gone as planned, the lab director would have had to pay an enormous penalty, and ownership of you would have gone to Bismark…
1000678255.korean: 뭐, 원래대로라면 연구소장님은 엄청난 위약금을 물고, 당신의 소유권 또한 비스마르크 쪽으로 넘어갔겠지만...

1000678256.english: But when Densetsu's chairman Yoshimitsu saw the script, he yelled, "CG for meteorites and volcanic eruptions? These idiots!" and canceled the collaboration between Densetsu and Bismark…
1000678256.korean: 덴세츠의 요시미츠 회장이 각본을 보더니, ‘운석이랑 화산 폭발 장면에 CG를 쓰겠다고? 이 멍청한 놈들!’ 이라고 노발대발하면서 덴세츠와 비스마르크의 협업을 취소했기 때문에...

1000678257.english: So the incident just fizzled out.
1000678257.korean: 사건은 어떻게 흐지부지 될 수 있었죠.

1000678259.english: Ha, I've been wondering all this time why you saved me…
1000678259.korean: 하, 여태까지 대체 왜 당신이 나를 살렸을까 고민하고 있었는데...

1000678260.english: It really was such a trivial reason.

1000678261.english: Wait, why did your voice get so gloomy?!

1000678262.english: Cheer up! That's proof that the lab director… well, in a unique way, valued you!
1000678262.korean: 기운 내세요! 그건 연구소장님이... 음, 독특한 방식이기는 했어도, 당신을 소중하게 생각했다는 증거잖아요!

1000678265.english: I just… got a little nostalgic for a moment.
1000678265.korean: 그냥, 잠깐 옛날 생각이 났을 뿐이야.

1000678266.english: Ugh, as expected of the Third Special Operations Unit. The AGS for security can't hold them off…
1000678266.korean: 윽, 역시 삼안 특작부대라고 해야하나. 경비용 AGS들이 버티질 못하네...

1000678268.english: Can't be helped. Brünhilde, you need to run.
1000678268.korean: 어쩔 수 없지. 브륀힐드, 너라도 도망쳐.

1000678269.english: What nonsense are you talking? Weren't we lab rats obligated to protect you?
1000678269.korean: 무슨 말도 안 되는 소리를 하고 있어. 우리 실험쥐들은 당신들을 지키는 게 의무 아니었나?

1000678270.english: Ah, seriously… who did you take after to be this stubborn?
1000678270.korean: 아, 진짜... 누굴 닮아서 말을 이렇게 안 듣는 거야...

1000678271.english: I never thought I'd feel like raising a child already, and I'm not even married yet.
1000678271.korean: 아직 결혼도 안 했는데 벌써부터 애 키우는 느낌을 받게 될 줄이야.

1000678272.english: I'm telling you to run! You… you…

1000678273.english: You're my creation. You can't just die here meaninglessly.
1000678273.korean: 내가 만든 작품이니까, 여기서 의미없이 죽어서는 안 돼.

1000678274.english: Then you certainly can't run. Wandering in the wasteland to starve is less meaningful than dying while protecting the human who made you, isn't it?
1000678274.korean: 그럼 더더욱 도망갈 수 없지. 황무지에서 떠돌아다니다가 아사하는 것보다는, 날 만든 인간을 지키다가 죽는 게 더 의미 있는 죽음 아니겠어?

1000678275.english: You stubborn fool… fine. If that's how you want to act…
1000678275.korean: 이 고집불통 멍청이 같으니... 좋아. 네가 그렇게 나온다면...

1000678276.english: Here. This is the data we extracted from our main computer.
1000678276.korean: 자. 여기, 우리 메인 컴퓨터에서 뽑아낸 데이터야.

1000678277.english: If you're a Bioroids from the Bermuda team, you know what's more important than the lab director, right?
1000678277.korean: 버뮤다 팀 소속 바이오로이드라면, 연구소장보다 더 중요한 게 뭔지 알겠지?

1000678279.english: Yeah. The data is the top priority to protect.

1000678280.english: So now, run. You can't hand this over to the Third Eye, can you?
1000678280.korean: 그러니까 이제 도망가. 삼안에 이걸 넘길 수는 없잖아?

1000678282.english: No matter what happens, get out with the data.
1000678282.korean: 무슨 일이 있어도, 데이터를 가지고 빠져나가.

1000678283.english: …Really, you're telling me to leave you like this?

1000678284.english: Yeah. At this rate of bleeding, you won't survive long anyway.
1000678284.korean: 그래. 어차피 이 정도 출혈이면 얼마 못 살아.

1000678285.english: …It's a shame. The wedding wasn't that far off…

1000678286.english: I wanted to show you me in a wedding dress.
1000678286.korean: 너한테 내가 웨딩드레스 입은 모습을 보여주고 싶었는데.

1000678287.english: You don't even have to. It wouldn't have suited you at all.
1000678287.korean: 안 봐도 뻔하지. 엄청나게 안 어울렸을 걸.

1000678288.english: Well… maybe. White doesn't really suit me.
1000678288.korean: 뭐... 그럴지도 모르지. 난 하얀색이 잘 안 받거든.

1000678290.english: …What, do you have something to say one last time?

1000678296.english: That's not what I wanted to hear.

1000678298.english: Of course. I told you, right? It was just some pointless old thought that came to mind.
1000678298.korean: 당연하지. 말했잖아? 그냥 쓸데없는 옛날 생각이 떠올랐을 뿐이라고.

1000678299.english: Want something to drink? When you're feeling down, it's good to have something warm…
1000678299.korean: 마실 거라도 드려요? 기분이 울적할 때는 따뜻한 거라도 마시면서...

1000678300.english: No. It's fine. I just… want to stand in the night air for a bit.
1000678300.korean: 아니. 됐어. 그냥... 잠깐 밤바람이나 맞고 있을래.

1000678302.english: …No, it's fine. I've had comfort from someone else before…
1000678302.korean: ...아니, 괜찮아. 이전에 다른 사람한테 위로를 받기도 했으니...

1000678303.english: I think it's better to be alone and sink into old memories tonight.
1000678303.korean: 오늘은 혼자 옛 추억에 젖어 있는 편이 나을 것 같아.

1000678305.english: The Chimera near London have all been dealt with…

1000678306.english: And one additional precaution has been taken.

1000678308.english: Yeah. We can't send electronic signals from here… but radio interference can't block old-style signals.
1000678308.korean: 응. 여기서는 전자 신호를 보낼 수 없지만... 전파 방해도 옛날 스타일 신호를 막을 수는 없거든.

1000678309.english: Old-style signals? What do you mean by that?
1000678309.korean: 옛날 스타일 신호라니? 그건 무슨 말이더냐?

1000678310.english: I set up signal fires on the rooftops nearby. Big enough to see from afar.
1000678310.korean: 근처 건물 옥상에 봉화를 설치하고 왔어. 멀리서도 볼 수 있을 정도로 크게 말이야.

1000678311.english: But, is this really okay? Isn't it too vague a method?
1000678311.korean: 그런데, 정말 이걸로 괜찮은 거 맞아? 너무 막연한 방법 아니야?

1000678312.english: Yeah. It's fine. We even added colored smoke.
1000678312.korean: 응. 괜찮아. 연기에 색을 내는 물질을 넣기까지 했으니까.

1000678314.english: Actually, according to the original plan, we should have returned yesterday.
1000678314.korean: 사실 원래 계획대로라면, 우리는 어제 귀환했어야 하거든.

1000678315.english: So everyone must be worried about us. They might even come to scout England.
1000678315.korean: 그러니까, 다들 우리를 걱정하고 있을 거란 말이지. 아마 영국을 정찰하러 올 지도 몰라.

1000678316.english: If they see those signal fires, they'll know we were in London.
1000678316.korean: 그때 저 봉화를 보게 된다면, 우리가 런던에 있었다는 걸 알게 되겠지.

1000678317.english: Will the signal fires last long?

1000678318.english: And what if the Chimera see the fires and swarm toward them…
1000678318.korean: 그리고, 혹시 철충들이 저 봉화를 보고 몰려든다면...

1000678319.english: Don't worry. I've planned obstacles along the Chimera's path.
1000678319.korean: 그건 걱정하지 마. 철충의 이동 경로에... 장애물을 배치할 생각이거든.

1000678320.english: Obstacles that can keep the fires burning for a long time.
1000678320.korean: 봉화를 오랫동안 태울 수 있는 종류의 장애물 말이야.

1000678321.english: Are you going to stay here?

1000678323.english: While you guys are struggling in Portsmouth fighting the AGS…
1000678323.korean: 뭐, 너희가 포츠머스에서 AGS들이랑 싸우면서 고생할 동안...

1000678324.english: I'll just relax here and burn some Chimera.
1000678324.korean: 난 여기서 편하게 철충이나 태우고 있으려고.

1000678325.english: But… it's going to be dangerous.

1000678326.english: It's dangerous there too, isn't it? This place might even be safer.
1000678326.korean: 거기도 위험하긴 마찬가지잖아? 오히려 여기가 더 안전할 수도 있단 말이지.

1000678327.english: So stop worrying about me and focus on what you need to do.
1000678327.korean: 그러니까, 내 걱정은 그만 두고 네 할 일이나 잘하라고.

1000678328.english: …Yes. I'll make sure we get a good result.

1000678330.english: …Alright, farewells are done, right? Our side is ready!
1000678330.korean: ...좋아, 작별 인사는 다 했지? 우리 쪽 준비는 다 끝났어!

1000678333.english: We're heading to Portsmouth to seize the Camelot!
1000678333.korean: 포츠머스로 가서, 카멜롯을 손에 넣는 거다!

1000678334.english: By the way, I didn't know you could operate a train.

1000678335.english: It really suits the name of a hero from Greek mythology!
