1000678335.korean: 그리스 신화 속 영웅의 이름과 참으로 잘 어울리는구나!

1000678336.english: Just as Greek heroes drove chariots into battle…
1000678336.korean: 그리스의 영웅들이 전차를 몰고 전장으로 향했듯이...

1000678337.english: We'll drive this train forward to victory, glory, and Portsmouth!
1000678337.korean: 우리는 열차를 몰고 승리와 영광, 그리고 포츠머스를 향해 진격하자!

1000678338.english: No, I don't actually know how to drive a train.
1000678338.korean: 아뇨, 열차 운전은 할 줄 모릅니다.

1000678340.english: Don't worry. I read the manual thoroughly an hour before departure.
1000678340.korean: 걱정하지 마세요. 출발하기 한 시간 전부터 매뉴얼을 정독했습니다.

1000678341.english: I understand everything about the train, so don't worry.
1000678341.korean: 기차의 모든 것을 파악해 두었으니, 걱정하지 마십시오.

1000678343.english: …Then tell me, what does this blue button do?
1000678343.korean: ...그럼 어디 한 번 말해보거라. 이 파란 버튼은 뭐 하는 버튼이지?

1000678344.english: I've mastered the theory necessary for driving.
1000678344.korean: 주행에 필요한 이론만큼은 완벽하게 숙지하고 있습니다.

1000678346.english: Saint of Jinzo, can you protect our allies with the power of light?
1000678346.korean: 진조의 성녀여, 빛의 힘으로 아군을 보호할 수 있느냐?

1000678348.english: I'm tough, so a train flipping over won't hurt me.
1000678348.korean: 전 튼튼하니까, 기차가 뒤집히는 것 정도의 충격에는 다치지 않아요.

1000678351.english: No, even if you are, what about us!
1000678351.korean: 아니, 네 녀석은 그렇다 쳐도, 우리는!

1000678352.english: I've prepared a requiem for the heroes. Do you want to hear it?

1000678358.english: I never agreed to this plan from the start…!
1000678358.korean: 애초부터 이 계획에 동의하는 게 아니었는데...!

1000678360.english: Did we just lose consciousness for a moment…

1000678363.english: Feels like I'm going to die from being crushed by the seatbelt…
1000678363.korean: 안전벨트에 눌려서 죽을 것 같긴 한데...

1000678366.english: Well, at least that's a relief.

1000678367.english: Thanks to the Chimera attacking the barricade and weakening it, the impact wasn't as severe as expected.
1000678367.korean: 철충이 바리케이드를 공격해서 바리케이드가 약화되어 있던 덕분에, 예상만큼 충격이 크진 않았어.

1000678368.english: …Wait, you mean it's normally even more intense than this?
1000678368.korean: ...잠깐, 원래는 이것보다 더 충격이 컸다는 말인가?

1000678369.english: We almost all died just now, didn't we?
1000678369.korean: 방금 우리 전부 죽을 뻔 한 거 아니야?

1000678371.english: Everything went exactly according to plan.

1000678373.english: Wait, I think I've been hearing a weird metallic sound since earlier…
1000678373.korean: 잠깐만, 근데 아까부터 이상한 금속음이 들려오는 것 같은데...

1000678374.english: Yeah. It sounds like metal scraping against metal…

1000678377.english: Oh no! The impact earlier derailed the tracks!

1000678378.english: If we keep going like this, the train might flip over!

1000678383.english: When modifying the train, I removed the brakes.

1000678385.english: The train didn't have enough speed to break through the barricade… so I removed the brakes and added an extra engine in that space.
1000678385.korean: 바리케이드를 돌파하기에는 기차의 속도가 충분하지 않아서... 브레이크를 떼어내고, 그 공간에 엔진을 하나 더 달았습니다.

1000678386.english: Since the collision with the barricade would slow us down, I calculated that even without brakes, we'd stop here.
1000678386.korean: 바리케이드와 충돌하면서 속도가 감소하니까, 브레이크가 없어도 여기서 멈춘다는 계산이 나왔거든요.

1000678388.english: …I didn't account for the barricade being shredded by the Chimera's attack.
1000678388.korean: ...철충의 공격으로 바리케이드가 너덜너덜한 상태일 것은 감안하지 못했습니다.

1000678389.english: Still, don't worry too much. We just need to increase the speed on the return track and break through the second barricade!
1000678389.korean: 그래도 너무 걱정하지 마세요. 역으로 속력을 더 높여서, 2차 바리케이드까지 뚫어버리면 됩니다!

1000678391.english: Everyone, fasten your seatbelts and hold tight!

1000678394.english: I should have realized something was off when we installed seatbelts on the train…
1000678394.korean: 기차에 안전벨트를 설치할 때부터 뭔가 이상하다는 걸 알아차렸어야 했는데...

1000678396.english: I'll never ride a train again. Really…
1000678396.korean: 다시는 기차 안 탈 거다. 진짜로...

1000678397.english: Haha… at least everyone's safe, that's a relief.

1000678405.english: …it's been so long since I last returned.

1000678406.english: Let's go. From here, I'll guide you. A blind guide is a bit strange, though…
1000678406.korean: 가시죠. 이제부터는 제가 안내하겠습니다. 장님이 길안내라니, 좀 이상하지만...

1000678409.english: Yes, well… thanks to how well you all fought.
1000678409.korean: 응, 뭐... 너희가 잘 싸운 덕분에.

1000678410.english: This time, the Chimera attacks were particularly fierce, weren't they?

1000678411.english: Yes. The Chimeras from London also gathered here… so the offensive was stronger.
1000678411.korean: 예. 런던에 있던 철충들도 여기로 몰려오고 있으니... 공세가 더 강해졌겠죠.

1000678414.english: We just ended up drawing more Chimeras because of us…
1000678414.korean: 괜히 저희 때문에, 철충을 더 끌어들인 셈이니...

1000678415.english: Eh, it's fine. They were already taking plenty of hits anyway.
1000678415.korean: 에이, 어차피 여기도 실컷 두들겨 맞는 중이었는데 뭐.

1000678416.english: Whether it's 10,000 or 20,000 Chimeras, it's about the same. Don't worry too much.
1000678416.korean: 철충이 1만마리가 오나, 2만 마리가 오나 비슷비슷해. 너무 신경 쓰지 말라고.

1000678417.english: If the enemy is overwhelmingly large, it's better to bring more reliable mercenaries. Right?
1000678417.korean: 어차피 상대가 압도적으로 많을 거라면, 그럴 바에는 든든한 용병들을 더 데려오는 편이 더 낫지. 안 그래?

1000678419.english: Yes, mercenaries I hired. Though I won't pay you.
1000678419.korean: 그럼, 내가 고용한 용병들이지. 돈은 안 줄 거지만 말이야.

1000678421.english: But at least I give you meals, right?

1000678422.english: Of course. Otherwise, you might have started a rebellion.
1000678422.korean: 그럼요. 안 그랬으면 반란이라도 일으켰을지도 몰라요?

1000678424.english: Well, I don't want to be kicked out either, so enjoy the benefits. Use the facilities freely, wash up, and don't worry about water.
1000678424.korean: 뭐, 나도 쫓겨나기는 싫으니까, 이 참에 좀 더 혜택을 즐기라고. 시설도 마음대로 쓰고, 씻으면서 물도 펑펑 쓰고 그래.

1000678425.english: …Wouldn't that be too much trouble for you?

1000678426.english: What does it matter? Friends can share a bit, right?
1000678426.korean: 뭐 어때, 친구끼리 같이 좀 쓸 수도 있지.

1000678428.english: Then! If we fought together, aren't we all friends?
1000678428.korean: 그럼! 같이 싸웠으면 다 친구 아니겠어?

1000678429.english: I haven't met them, but our captain would surely say the same.
1000678429.korean: 만나본 적은 없지만, 우리 대장님도 분명 그렇게 말할 걸.

1000678430.english: "If we fight together, we're either rivals or friends, why make it so complicated?" that's what they'd say.
1000678430.korean: ‘같이 싸우면 다 라이벌 아니면 친구지, 뭐 그렇게 복잡하게 따져?’ 라고 말이야.

1000678431.english: So, whether you want to take a bath, or play in the water… do as you like.
1000678431.korean: 그러니까, 목욕을 하던, 아니면 물놀이를 하던... 마음대로 해.

1000678434.english: <The Blind Princess and the Land of Mist> Part 2 continues.

1000678435.english: Ugh, well, yeah. There's a lot to do.
1000678435.korean: 으으, 뭐 그렇지. 할 일이 많잖아.

1000678436.english: If Mari says so, should we take a short break?

1000678437.english: By the way, what's going on?

1000678438.english: Good idea. Do you have something in mind?

1000678439.english: Alright, then discuss it with Alpha and Armand…
1000678439.korean: 그래, 그럼 알파와 아르망과 논의한 다음…

1000678442.english: Alright, then we land in England and search for the missing…
1000678442.korean: 좋아, 그럼 영국에 상륙해서 실종자를 찾고...

1000678444.english: How did the situation end up like this?

1000678446.english: What? Isn't that their own territory?

1000678448.english: If that's the case, sending in troops would be risky.

1000678450.english: Then let's try to find a way.

1000678451.english: An executive-level member of Black River.

1000678452.english: We'll need a map of the British Black River military facilities.
1000678452.korean: 아무래도 영국의 블랙리버 군사시설 배치도가 필요하겠는데.

1000678456.english: No, we just returned from the operation. Rest a bit more.
1000678456.korean: 아니, 작전에서 돌아온 지 얼마 안 되었잖아. 좀 더 쉬어.

1000678457.english: Be careful. Calais is still within Delta's territory.

1000678459.english: Will there be no issues passing through?

1000678460.english: Good, I think we've seen everything we needed to. Return carefully.
1000678460.korean: 좋아, 확인해야 할 건 다 본 것 같네. 조심해서 돌아와.

1000678462.english: That means, "I'm fine, so you be careful too and don't get hurt."
1000678462.korean: 그건 ‘난 괜찮으니까 너도 안 다치게 조심해’ 라는 뜻이야.

1000678463.english: I'm just mediating in case there's a misunderstanding.
1000678463.korean: 오해가 있을까 봐 중재해 주는 것 뿐이야.

1000678466.english: Don't worry, I'm sure they aren't really mad. Instead, could you call Lian?
1000678466.korean: 뭐, 진짜 화난 건 아닐 테니까 걱정 마. 그보다, 리앤 좀 불러줄래?

1000678467.english: Brünhilde has a reason she must join the rescue team.
1000678467.korean: 브륀힐드는 구출팀에 합류해야만 하는 이유가 있어.

1000678472.korean: 그래, 이건 어쩔 수 없는 일이야...

1000678473.english: We need to build a temporary base for the rescue team, so we'll need materials there…
1000678473.korean: 구출팀을 위해 임시 기지를 건설해야 하니까, 거기 필요한 물자를...

1000678475.english: We're not working! We're just going to play Steel Line Online!
1000678475.korean: 일하는 게 아니야! 스틸라인 온라인을 하려고 그러는 거지!

1000678479.english: …Without a special reason, it's hard to allow.

1000678485.english: …No. I don't know. I can't just guess.

1000678486.english: It's better to hear that directly from them.
1000678486.korean: 그건 아무래도 본인에게 직접 들어보는 편이 좋겠는데.

1000678487.english: Go carefully. Look after the other team members too.
1000678487.korean: 조심해서 다녀와. 다른 대원들도 잘 지켜주고.

1000678488.english: I'm quite a decent counselor, you know. No license, but still.
1000678488.korean: 내가 꽤 괜찮은 상담사거든. 자격증은 없지만.

1000678489.english: Uh… it's a bit cold. I didn't plan to come outside. Should I go in?
1000678489.korean: 어... 좀 춥긴 하네. 밖에 나올 생각은 없었는데. 들어갈까?

1000678490.english: Yeah. So don't worry too much.

1000678492.english: - With Phantom, such accidents can be avoided as much as possible.
1000678492.korean: - 팬텀이 있으면 그런 불상사는 최대한 피할 수 있을 것이다.

1000678494.english: Commander, you'll love Momo no matter what she looks like, right? Whether it's when the moon is full of power, when it's been touched by the power of the black moon, or even in this current form. Hehe! That's the same for Momo too! Commander! May you live a long and happy life together. No, a thousand years of happiness. No, let's do it for eons!
1000678494.korean: 사령관님은 모모가 어떤 모습이더라도 사랑해 주시는 거죠? 달의 힘이 가득할 때도, 검은 달의 힘을 받았을 때도, 그리고 지금 이 모습일 때도. 헤헤! 그건 모모도 마찬가지예요! 사령관님! 백년해로. 아니, 천년해로. 아니, 억겁해로하도록 해요!

1000678495.english: I swear on the moon-shaped ring I received from the gentleman. Just as the moon is still the moon no matter where or when, I am still me no matter where or when I go. Anywhere, anytime... I will love you, sir, unchangingly.
1000678495.korean: 신사에게 받은, 달처럼 둥근 반지에 맹세하나니. 달은 어느 곳, 어느 시간에도 여전히 달인 것처럼, 저는 어느 곳을 가도, 어느 시간이든 저예요. 어느 곳, 어느 시간이든... 변치 않고 신사를 사랑하겠어요.

1000678496.english: In the past, after the munitions factory destruction operation ended

1000678498.english: No, well... we didn't really do much.
1000678498.korean: 아뇨, 저희야 뭐... 별로 한 게 없죠.

1000678499.english: In the end, it was Princess and the AGSs who dealt with the Iron Bug, wasn't it?
1000678499.korean: 결국 철충을 처리하신 건 프린세스 씨와 AGS들이잖아요?

1000678500.english: Of course, it’s because you, the resistance fighters, are giving us strong support from behind.
1000678500.korean: 그야, 저항군 여러분이 저희 뒤를 든든하게 받쳐 주니까요.

1000678501.english: That is why you can fight with peace of mind.
1000678501.korean: 그래서 안심하고 싸울 수 있는 거에요.

1000678504.english: Right, right. Since the Bio-Roids are holding off the Iron Bug in front, the AGSs can form a firing line with peace of mind, without worrying about infection.
1000678504.korean: 그래, 그래. 앞에서 바이오로이드들이 철충을 막아주니까, AGS들이 감염 걱정 없이 마음 놓고 화망을 형성할 수 있는 거란 말이지.

1000678508.english: Don't be fooled, Leprechaun. That's Merlin.

1000678509.english: How did you know? Who is the spy! Is it Cerberus? Or Safety?
1000678509.korean: 어떻게 알았지? 첩자는 누구냐! 켈베로스냐? 아니면 세이프티?

1000678510.english: Surely it’s not my loyal henchman Vanilla!
1000678510.korean: 설마 내 충실한 심복 바닐라는 아니겠지!

1000678511.english: Of course, you can easily tell even without a spy. Mr. Pubhead doesn't use such a frivolous tone.
1000678511.korean: 그야 첩자가 없이도 쉽게 알 수 있지요. 펍헤드 씨는 그렇게 경박한 말투를 안 쓰거든요.

1000678512.english: What? Frivolous? I'm just going to...

1000678514.english: Just... yank him down and... pound him... Argh, seriously! He won't go down!
1000678514.korean: 그냥... 확... 자빠뜨려서 파운딩을... 아잇, 진짜! 안 넘어가잖아!

1000678515.english: Hehe, as a comrade of the Dragon Slayer, I can't lose a test of strength.
1000678515.korean: 후훗, 용살자의 동료인 제가 힘 싸움에서 질 수는 없죠.

1000678518.english: Argh! The speaker is breaking! Surrender! Surrender!

1000678519.english: Hehe, you two really get along well.
1000678519.korean: 후훗, 두 분은 정말 사이가 좋으시네요.

1000678520.english: ...I don't know where you mean we're on good terms.
